races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Ballinea Star
runners.spotlight: Made 26,000euros as a yearling; showed speed for a long way on debut at Naas over 6f on good before fading; improved dropped to 5f at Navan last time when a close fourth, and subsequent form of those in front is good; returns to 6f now but she's in the mix.
runners.comment: Improved over debut when fourth dropped to 5f last time in good race; returns to 6f now

runners.name: Madam Secretary
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly was not particularly strong in the market when a 5l third of eight for her debut at Listowel (6.5f, good); no surprise to see her improve over that initial outing.
runners.comment: No surprise to see the Wootton Bassett filly improve over her 5l third on debut
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Saoirse Silk
runners.spotlight: 150,000euros yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to winners Motazzen (10.5f-1m6f Flat inc AW, RPR 90; 2m-2m5f hurdle) and Crystal Mer (2m/2m4f hurdle); dam French 1m3f and 1m4f (Listed) winner (107), half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Matterhorn; third in 6f Naas barrier trial last month behind the winner, Argentarius; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 150,000euros yearling; third in a barrier trial last month

runners.name: Carafinn
runners.spotlight: 120,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Warriors Whisper (French Group-placed 9.5f AW; RPR 98) and Carencey (6f/7f; 82); dam 7f-1m2f winner (inc Group 3; 105), sister to dam of top-class middle-distance performer Pyledriver; finished fourth in a 6f Naas barrier trial last month, won by Argentarius (Saoirse Silk in third); could get involved.
runners.comment: 120,000euros yearling; third in a barrier trial won by Argentarius; could get involved

runners.name: Fine Line
runners.spotlight: 26,000euros foal, 50,000euros yearling; second foal; dam unplaced 1m-1m4f (RPR 58), half-sister to winners Hypothetical (UAE 1m2f Group 1) and Pourquoi Pas (French 9.5f), out of Group-placed 1m2f/10.5f winning half-sister to French 1,000 Guineas winner Precieuse; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 50,000euros yearling; dam unplaced 1m-1m4f (RPR 58); others more appealing

runners.name: Scandi Noir
runners.spotlight: Dam placed 5f/6f (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 80), half-sister to prolific 5f-6f winner Brian The Snail and 5f 2yo Listed winner Shadow Hunter, out of Danish 1m winning half-sister to 1,000 Guineas third Super Sleuth; others appeal more on paper.
runners.comment: Dam placed 5f/6f (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 80); others appeal more on paper

runners.name: Argentarius
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Good American (7f 2yo/10.5f; RPR 105), Falls Of Acharn (1m Flat, 83; 1m7f/2m hurdle) and Sweet Baby Zou (9.5f; 68); dam German 1m-1m4f winner (inc Group 3; 106); winner of a 6f Naas barrier trial last month (Saoirse Silk and Carafinn behind)
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners; winner of a 6f Naas barrier trial last month; check market

runners.name: Back To The Lodge
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 8.6f AW winner (RPR 74), half-sister to Listed winners Jabaara (7f) and Tajanis (Australian 1m4f), out of Listed-placed 7f winner; others preferred.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; dam 8.6f AW winner (RPR 74); others preferred

runners.name: Ocean Surge
runners.spotlight: (120,000euros vendor yearling); third foal; dam French 1m-1m3f maiden, half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Barsanti, out of Listed-placed 1m 2yo winning sister to champion 2yo Xaar; check market with McMonagle aboard (5-18 riding for this trainer).
runners.comment: (120,000euros vendor yearling); McMonagle rides for her debut

runners.name: New Bond Street
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling, 200,000gns 2yo; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Cover Point (5f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 95), Snuggle (6f; 84) and Star Marian (5f AW; 64); dam 5f/6f winner (102), half-sister to 5f/6f winner Weekend Escape, out of 5f Listed winner; trainer does well at this track and she looks like a big contender.
runners.comment: Purchased for 200,000gns in April; trainer does well here; big contender.

runners.name: Sapphire Petal
runners.spotlight: Blue Point filly; 95,000euros yearling (190,000gns vendor 2yo); third foal; closely related to French 1m winner Take Me On (inc 2yo; RPR 100), half-sister to 6f-1m winner Art Market (inc 2yo/France; 87); dam 5f/6f winner (inc 2yo/AW; 97), half-sister to smart 6f/7f winner Ask Frank; worth a market check.
runners.comment: (190,000gns vendor 2yo); closely related to French 1m winner Take Me On (RPR 100)

runners.name: Siege Of Tyre
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Xian Express (5f-1m inc AW/Greece; RPR 82), Wind Of Dubai (Singapore 5.5f/6f) and Giddyupadingdong (bumper); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (87); third of nine in a Naas 7f barrier trial last month; others preferred.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two Flat winners; third in a 7f barrier trial last month; others preferred

runners.name: Shafters Legacy
runners.spotlight: Towards rear on 5f debut here (soft); improvement needed.
runners.comment: Towards rear on 5f debut here (soft); improvement needed

runners.name: Dropping Dimes
runners.spotlight: Huge odds for her Curragh debut and ran like it; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Huge odds for her Curragh debut and ran like it; look elsewhere

runners.name: Thatsanotherstory
runners.spotlight: Never got involved at the Curragh over 6f (good to yielding); much more needed.
runners.comment: Never got involved at the Curragh over 6f (good to yielding); much more needed

races.race_name: Mallow Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Collecting Coin
runners.spotlight: Consistent form last season included a Galway maiden win (7f, yielding); good efforts in three handicaps this term, most recently when a 3.75l third over C&D last time, when a high draw was a disadvantage; cheekpieces go on now for just his fourth handicap.
runners.comment: Has run with credit in three h'caps this season inc' C&D last time

runners.name: Bishopton
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last year over 7f, winning twice; resumed progress on return in March when defying top-weight at Dundalk off 84; cheekpieces worn when well beaten behind some of these rivals at the Curragh (6f, good) have been abandoned.
runners.comment: Progressed well last term; well beaten behind some of these last time
runners.owner: W Jones/D W Sloan/M C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Aviatrice
runners.spotlight: Ended last year on a roll with successive AW wins at Dundalk, the second occasion a fillies' Listed contest over 1m; posted a cracking seasonal return at the Curragh (6f, good) last time when a 0.75l third, behind winner Headmaster; retains same mark, 7lb below AW one; big player.
runners.comment: Ended last year on a roll and posted a cracking third on seasonal return; big player

runners.name: Indigo Dream
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 12 starts came on the AW over this trip, but has been runner-up four times on turf, including at Naas over 6f (Go Athletico third); denied a clear run at the Curragh last time (6f, good) when behind a couple of these; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Sole win came on AW but has been runner-up multiple times on turf; denied clear run latest

runners.name: Bint Majestic Roi
runners.spotlight: In the hunt for a three-timer following back-to-back wins of a 21-runner handicap at the Curragh in April (6f, soft) and a 6f contest at Naas the following month (good), needed to wait for room before winning by a short head; she's up an aggregate of 18lb but is in wonderful form.
runners.comment: Hunting for a three-timer following back-to-back wins; up 18lb in total but in good form

runners.name: L L Koulsty
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 6f maiden here last September (yielding to soft); acquitted himself well in opening two handicap runs this season before landing a C&D handicap last month (soft to heavy), keeping on well; would probably prefer softer and he's 8lb wrong.
runners.comment: M'den winner here added a C&D h'cap last time; would prefer softer and 8lb wrong

runners.name: Key Witness
runners.spotlight: Arrives in search of a fourth straight win following victories in big-field handicaps at the Curragh in November (7f, heavy) and on seasonal return in April (1m, soft to heavy), continued progression when successful over C&D last month (soft), ahead of Collecting Coin and Gleneagle Bay; up another 5lb and better ground raises a question.
runners.comment: Added third straight win last time over C&D; ground a question mark but he's progressive

runners.name: Fort Vega
runners.spotlight: Better than ever last summer, winning over C&D (good) and career-best in Scurry Handicap at the Curragh over an extended 6.5f in July; matched that effort when beaten just a neck by Headmaster in the Habitat Handicap back at the Curragh on seasonal return (6f, good); entitled to improve and he's in the mix off 2lb higher.
runners.comment: C&D winner was beaten just a head by Headmaster on seasonal return; could improve

runners.name: Barbapapa
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner who sole turf success from eight starts came over 7f at Leopardstown in 2023 off 65; ran better than finishing position suggests on Curragh seasonal return and entitled to improve; 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: Sole turf win came in 2023; entitled to improve over comeback run; 4lb wrong.

runners.name: Rahmi
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner (three over this trip); has struggled in Listed company in two runs this term; returns to handicap company now and just 3lb above last winning mark but would prefer to see him come here in better form, given he's the top weight.
runners.comment: Struggled in both Listed runs this term; tough task off top weight
runners.owner: Richard McNally & Leo Walsh & L Murrin

runners.name: Sir Les Patterson
runners.spotlight: All five wins have come on the AW, including two wins at 6f over the winter; creditable efforts in two recent turf handicaps, including when midfield in the Habitat at the Curragh (6f, good) when slowly away; 12lb lower mark compared to AW and up in trip now.
runners.comment: Five-time AW winner has run creditably in two recent turf h'caps; 12lb lower turf mark

runners.name: Engines On
runners.spotlight: Bookended last season with 7f wins at Naas (yielding) in July and at the Curragh in September (soft); two Curragh runs this term have been underwhelming but he has dropped down to 4lb above his last winning mark; new headgear combination tried now.
runners.comment: Bookened last season with 7f wins at Naas and the Curragh; poor in two starts this term

runners.name: City House
runners.spotlight: Smart turf form at up to 7f in Bahrain for George Scott early last year; some good runs for current yard this year, second to Sir Les Patterson on the AW (6f) in February and close fourth at Chester in May (7.5f, good); caught the eye when running on late after not getting a clear run in the Habitat at the Curragh (6f, good); could go close.
runners.comment: Good Bahrain form; eyecatcher in Habitat last time when running on after denied clear run

runners.name: Headmaster
runners.spotlight: Added to dual British 7f wins when bagging the Habitat handicap (6f, good) at the Curragh last month on second start for new yard, struggled to find a gap before blasting home to win by a neck (ahead of many of these); 5lb rise seems fair and this trip is fine; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Blasted home to beat many of these last time; 5lb rise fair and has won over 7f; shortlist

runners.name: Gleneagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on debut at Dundalk in 2024; 0-14 since but ran well in top handicaps last season, including third in Victoria Cup at Ascot and second in Ahonoora at Galway; solid C&D fourth in May (soft) but didn't build on that in a similar contest last time; down 3lb but needs more; Colin Keane rides for the first time.
runners.comment: Good runs in defeat in top h'caps last term; decent C&D 4th before lesser effort latest

runners.name: Go Athletico
runners.spotlight: Nine-time turf winner, including a Group 3 and Listed contests, was once rated 108 so has dropped a long way; positive third at Naas in April (6f, good); put a line through his latest Curragh run where he got no run; 3lb wrong but not ruled out.
runners.comment: At one time rated 108; positive third on penultimate; put a line through latest run

runners.name: Bella Isabella
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 7f on the round course last season (good) and took third of eight in a Leopardstown Group 3 (7f, good) next time; much improved display over seasonal return when winning by a nose at Leopardstown over this trip (7f, soft) ten days ago; carries 7lb penalty but there could be improvement in her.
runners.comment: M'den winner here last term; won over this trip last time; 7lb penalty but could improve

races.race_name: Follow Cork Racecourse On Social Media Maiden

runners.name: Xerling
runners.spotlight: Markedly improved over three juvenile runs when a close second over 5f at Navan (yielding), pulling clear of the remainder; that run marks her out as a big player and McMonagle rides (3-5 riding for this trainer).
runners.comment: Top run when a close second over 5f on seasonal debut; big player

runners.name: Sparky Sparky
runners.spotlight: Standout run came on seasonal return over C&D (soft) when a staying on third; ran no race here next time before a fair fourth on Dundalk AW in a tongue-tie (now left off); 3.5l eighth of 12 on handicap debut at the Curragh last time; tried in cheekpieces now.
runners.comment: Standout run came over C&D on seasonal return but lesser runs since

runners.name: Cactus
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly is 0-10; ran a nice race in a Curragh maiden on seasonal return (6f, soft); way below that level at Down Royal next time (7f, good); hard to be confident and now ridden by 10lb claimer.
runners.comment: 0-10; didn't build on positive seasonal return last time; hard to be confident

runners.name: Grande Cuvee
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; too keen at the Curragh when weakening into a 10l ninth of 24; better effort at Navan (5f, yielding) when 5.5l behind Xerling; needs to bridge that gap.
runners.comment: Finished 5.5l behind Xerling at Navan and needs to bridge the gap

runners.name: Osterode
runners.spotlight: Sister to 5f/6f winner Ostraka (inc AW Group 3; RPR 96), half-sister to 5f/6f winner Charlemont (inc HK; 88); dam Listed-placed 5f winner (inc AW; 98), half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Osterhase; visor on for debut and worth a look in the market.
runners.comment: Sister to 5f/6f winner Ostraka (RPR 96); visor on for debut; check market

runners.name: In The Gloaming
runners.spotlight: Last of 12 on debut at the Curragh in September and forced much of the pace on seasonal return before fading on soft ground at Gowran last month; probably more one for handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Finished last on debut and faded on seasonal return; may do better in h'caps
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.name: Riot Star
runners.spotlight: Starman gelding; half-brother to winners Bell I Am (Listed-placed 5.7f 2yo; RPR 80), Sonemoss (1m; 80) and Deft Touch (Swedish 8.7f-11.5f); dam 7f winner (80), closely related to 6f Group 3 2yo winner Princess Iris, out of 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; finished last in a Leopardstown barrier trial last August; blinkers on.
runners.comment: Starman gelding finished last in a barrier trial last August.

runners.name: Frosted Sky
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never in contention on 5f AW debut; improved effort when eighth of 18 at the Curragh last time(6.5f, good to yielding); others more appealing.
runners.comment: Improved over debut when eighth of 18 at the Curragh last time; others preferred

runners.name: You Smiled
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in opening two maidens here (6f, soft) and at Navan (5f, yielding); tongue-tie on now.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in opening two maidens inc' here; tongue tie on now

runners.name: Finika
runners.spotlight: Never involved at the Curragh over 6.5f on debut; hard to fancy after that.
runners.comment: Never involved at the Curragh over 6.5f on debut; hard to fancy after that

runners.name: West Sedona
runners.spotlight: Beaten 11l in a 7f Gowran claimer on debut (good to yielding); best watched.
runners.comment: Beaten 11l in a 7f Gowran claimer on debut (good to yielding); best watched

runners.name: McHenry Sunrise
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; showed more in her second maiden start at Navan over 5f when shaping as though further would suit; hood worn that day is retained.
runners.comment: Showed more in second m'den when shaping like further would suit

runners.name: Little Roy
runners.spotlight: Looked inexperienced at Sligo over 6.5f and well beaten at 200-1 at the Curragh last time; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Moderate form shown in two m'dens to date; hard to fancy

runners.name: Kula Bula Boyo
runners.spotlight: Faded after racing prominently on debut over 7f here (soft to heavy) and similar story at the Curragh (6.5f, good to yielding); blinkers go on but much more required.
runners.comment: Faded in both m'dens to date; drops in trip now but much more required

runners.name: Ballerina Dancer
runners.spotlight: Massive odds and no sign of winning potential in three maidens; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Massive odds and no sign of winning potential in three maidens; look elsewhere

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Midsummer Sprint Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Won her maiden over C&D on good ground last year for Jack Davison; an improved performer this season, winning twice (including back here) and outrunning her odds of 50-1 in a 6f Group 3 at Naas four weeks ago; races prominently and could prove tough to reel in back at 5f, for all that she's in against her elders this time.
runners.comment: Near miss in a Group 3 last time and could prove tough to reel in back at 5f

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: Nearly won a Curragh handicap before her two good runs at Listed level at Naas in races won by the smart Mission Central, finishing closer on the first occasion; she was only a half-length behind Keke the last time (both behind Gazelle d'Or) and they're all on the same terms.
runners.comment: Only a half-length behind Keke the last time and they're on the same terms
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Keke
runners.spotlight: Multiple sprint winner who enjoys good ground and finished third (behind Gazelle d'Or) in his first Listed race at Naas last month despite an unfavourable track position; looks sure to go well.
runners.comment: Third in his first Listed race at Naas last month despite an unfavourable track position

runners.name: Fregada
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly who won a Curragh 6f maiden on debut at two; showed up well for a long way in a 6f Group 2 back there three weeks ago and she's down in class here and trip; hard to rule out.
runners.comment: Good run in a Group 2 last time; she's down in class here and trip; hard to rule out

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 5-21 and better than ever in her last two races, finishing second from a good draw at Naas (5f Listed; some of these behind) before posting the same RPR over 6f at Fairyhouse; in the mix.
runners.comment: Strike-rate of 5-21 and better than ever in her last two races; very much in the mix

runners.name: Eclairage
runners.spotlight: Has only won on the AW but nearly put that right in a 5f handicap at Navan last month and probably resented the rain-hit ground when well beaten in last week's Dash at Epsom; has bit to find on these terms, though.
runners.comment: Forgiven last week's effort at a wet Epsom; has bit to find on these terms, though

runners.name: Billie Be Quick
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner in handicaps; has finished behind Keke in her last two races but wasn't too far away last time at Naas (Listed) despite fluffing the start; 80-1 that day but won't be anything like those odds here; could feature.
runners.comment: Not far behind Keke last time at Naas despite fluffing the start

runners.name: Kendall Roy
runners.spotlight: Has a chance according to official ratings but twice disappointed in Bahrain after winning a 5f handicap there in December and cheekpieces now go on; the break shouldn't be an issue as he's able when fresh.
runners.comment: Chance on the figures and the break shouldn't be an issue as he's able when fresh
runners.owner: Miss Kate McGivern & Thomas Hore & Shane M Power

runners.name: Erosandpsyche
runners.spotlight: Ran perfectly well off 96 in a Navan handicap last week (5f, good) but he's without a win since 2022 and looks vulnerable on these terms.
runners.comment: Ran well last week but on a losing run and looks vulnerable on these terms

races.race_name: Ryan's Cleaning Handicap

runners.name: Fox In Flight
runners.spotlight: Could make a case on last season's exploits but he's failed to beat a rival in his two handicaps this year, both over 5f.
runners.comment: Could make a case on last season's exploits but failed to beat a rival in his handicaps

runners.name: Treasured Royal
runners.spotlight: Highly tried to no avail after winning a Navan maiden last season; firmly back on track in a new hood when third at Naas (6f) but wasn't as good at Fairyhouse; capable off this mark, but not entirely sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Capable off this mark but her first run in the hood was much better than the second

runners.name: Happy Henry
runners.spotlight: Consistent gelding who raced freely in the new cheekpieces last week at Navan (5f) but still prevailed in a tight finish; respected, for all that an 8lb rise was hefty.
runners.comment: Won in the new cheekpieces last week despite taking a strong hold; raised 8lb

runners.name: Beauty Queen
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Kevin Ryan but 0-12 for current yard and recent efforts are none too persuasive.
runners.comment: Won twice for Kevin Ryan but 0-12 for current yard and recent efforts none too persuasive

runners.name: Cuban Grey
runners.spotlight: Brought up the rear last week at Navan but that was a tougher race than this and he had previously posted a seventh career win over 5f at Dundalk (3lb lower); no surprise to see him run well.
runners.comment: Brought up the rear last week at Navan but that was a tougher race than this

runners.name: Genesis
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner who got off the mark on turf in a 6f Curragh handicap last month (good; 3lb lower); did well to be a close fourth next time after getting upset in the stalls and was reportedly tight for room in his last race; respected off what's still a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Two excellent runs before getting tight for room in his last race; competitive mark

runners.name: Lamar Canyon
runners.spotlight: First run since being gelded when showing much improved form to win a 5f maiden on the AW; can pick holes in the form (placed horses beaten since) but he might be progressive.
runners.comment: Can pick holes in his maiden win but that was his first run since being gelded

runners.name: Nouvel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year off the very same mark but, bar his second at Naas in May, he's not been so clever this season.
runners.comment: Won this race last year off the very same mark but might not be in the same form now
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over 5f on varying ground but hasn't been at her best so far this season and sluggish starts have become a habit.
runners.comment: Below par this season and sluggish starts have become a habit
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien

runners.name: Harry's Hill
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner at this trip but hasn't been close to his best for a quite a while now; well behind Happy Henry last weekend.
runners.comment: Multiple winner at this trip but hasn't been close to his best for a quite a while now

runners.name: Devil's Angel
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner but well held in three runs since moving yards and has been off since September; now a 10yo.
runners.comment: Well held in three runs since moving yards and has been off since September; now a 10yo

races.race_name: Darley Munster Oaks Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Beset
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on a high with a 1m4f Listed win at Naas (heavy); maintained progression back at scene of her win on return (10.5f, soft); close second behind stablemate Starford in a 1m2f Curragh Group 3 in April (soft); consistency took a hit upon encountering good ground in a Curragh Group 2 last time, when a well-beaten fifth of six; wants softer than this and McMonagle rides Nyra.
runners.comment: Seen to good effect on softer ground, winning dual Listed races; McMonagle rides Nyra
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Nyra
runners.spotlight: Bought for 875,000euros at Arqana in December; 1-9 for former yard in Germany, winning a 1m3f Hamburg Group 3 (good) and better form when beaten a close third in the German Oaks at Dusseldorf (1m3f, soft) and placed twice in two Group 2 races afterwards; creditable Irish/stable debut when fourth behind easy winner Purview over this trip at the Curragh (good); McMonagle remains loyal and could improve plenty; big player.
runners.comment: German Gr.3 winner and placed at Gr.1 level; could improve plenty from Irish debut

runners.name: Eretria
runners.spotlight: Beaten 0.75l by stablemate Emmeleia (runner-up in Group 3 next time) at Dundalk (10.5f) before bolting up at Leopardstown next time (1m2f, yielding); didn't progress as expected when failing to justify favouritism in a Naas Listed race last time (10.5f, good); holds an Irish Oaks entry but must prove she stays the trip now and resume progress.
runners.comment: Bolted up in second m'den; didn't progress as expected when beaten fav' latest

runners.name: Sparan Nua
runners.spotlight: Arrives unbeaten following two wins in fillies' company; nicely won her Naas maiden at 25-1 (1m, good) and confirmed her ability when showing a good turn of foot in a slowly-run Gowran conditions race (9.5f good to yielding); tackles new trip and needs to take another step forward, but hasn't put a foot wrong yet.
runners.comment: 2-2 so far; new trip and another step forward needed but hasn't put a foot wrong yet

runners.name: Perfect Your Craft
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of her six races for Ralph Beckett; purchased for 300,000gns and posted a cracking effort on seasonal return/stable debut when keeping on well over 1m2f in a big-field premier handicap at the Curragh, denied by a nose; enters Group 3 company for the first time but worthy of consideration with improvement; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: 3-6 for Ralph Beckett; cracking run on seasonal return/stable debut; enters Gr.3 ranks now

runners.name: Bloom
runners.spotlight: Won her maiden at the second attempt over 1m here in September (yielding); good front-running third of seven in a Navan Group 3 on seasonal return (1m2f, good to yielding); didn't build on that when 7.5l behind stablemate Cameo in Lingfield Listed company last month (11.5f, good to firm); no surprise to see her do better with blinkers now applied.
runners.comment: 1m m'den winner here last term; decent third on return but didn't build on that last time
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Jannah Star
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but ran a decent race in a 1m6f Gowran Listed race (good to yielding) last time when sixth, ahead of higher rated rivals; up to a rating of 88 now, but trainers' other two candidates make more appeal.
runners.comment: 0-7 but ran a decent race when sixth in Listed company latest; others preferred

runners.name: Ice Dancer
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner here and Group 3-placed over 1m on heavy in the autumn; reportedly showed signs of coming into season when 26l behind on Group 3 seasonal return at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding); few apparent excuses when 18.5l behind in a Naas Listed contest last time (10.5f, good); holds an Irish Oaks entry, but Bloom is more appealing of trainer's two; blinkers on.
runners.comment: 1m maiden winner here on hvy last season; last in both runs this term; others preferred
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bosphorus Rose
runners.spotlight: Productive 2025 campaign for Sir Mark Prescott included a 1m4f handicap hat-trick; signed off from former yard well beaten in Listed company on AW at Lingfield (1m5f); purchased for 150,000gns in the meantime and has the rating to get involved, but trainer's other runner, Perfect Your Craft, is arguably more interesting.
runners.comment: Hat-trick of 1m4f h'cap wins last term in Britain; could get involved

runners.name: Marazion
runners.spotlight: Gained first win at the eighth attempt in a Roscommon fillies' maiden last month (1m4f, good); nice entry to handicap company at the Curragh last time when second of 24 (1m6f, good); big career best needed here all the same.
runners.comment: 1-9 with win coming in fillies' m'den on penultimate; big career best needed

races.race_name: Racing Again Friday 10th July Handicap

runners.name: Chirac
runners.spotlight: Built on his encouraging handicap debut to win at Gowran (7f, soft; 7lb lower) but he never gave favourite backers any hope when failing to fire at Leopardstown (1m, soft to heavy); has to rebound from that.
runners.comment: Gowran winner but never gave favourite backers any hope last time at Leopardstown

runners.name: Schoolyard Days
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form at sprint trips; midfield in a 7.5f claimer before finishing well behind in a Curragh handicap over shorter; good bit to prove, including her stamina for this far.
runners.comment: Weak form thus far and has a good bit to prove, including her stamina for this far

runners.name: Cause I Like You
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has hinted at winning potential on the AW but has produced two modest runs on grass; others safer.
runners.comment: Winning potential on the AW but has produced two modest runs on grass; others safer

runners.name: Stella Amorosa
runners.spotlight: Some promise last season in maidens; weak 20-1 chance when down the field on her handicap debut, but this is an easier grade and cheekpieces go on; may show more.
runners.comment: Tenth on handicap debut but this is an easier grade and cheekpieces go on; may show more

runners.name: Cisterna
runners.spotlight: 1-13, the win coming over slightly shorter at Roscommon last month (good; 5lb lower); however, only midfield at the Curragh since and his form is very up and down.
runners.comment: Roscommon winner last month off 5lb lower; his form is very up and down

runners.name: Clonbun
runners.spotlight: His win came in a claimer (1m, soft; first-time blinkers) but was third of 13 in a good-ground handicap at Roscommon and has been gelded since then; that could trigger further progress.
runners.comment: Claiming winner, then third in a handicap; now gelded, may improve some more

runners.name: Hambelton
runners.spotlight: Only had six races and was sent off favourite for the latest at Haydock (1m, good to soft) where he pulled too hard in a reapplied hood (now removed); didn't run too badly all things considered and there's a race in him off this mark.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard when favourite at Haydock and there's a race in him off this mark

runners.name: Push Harder
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive at the end of last year on the AW and perhaps needed the run on her comeback; should be fine on turf and this lightly-raced filly can't be dismissed despite being 3lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: This lightly raced filly can't be dismissed despite being 3lb out of the weights

runners.name: Bear Right
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; up with the pace and kept on for third at Leoparstown ten days ago (1m, soft to heavy), finishing some 7l off the clearcut winner; looks to have found her level.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; up with the pace and kept on for third at Leoparstown ten days ago

runners.name: Cameclose
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 5l in his two handicaps at around 1m3f when stamina was perhaps an issue; drops in trip with cheekpieces tried and he's off a 3lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Beaten about 5l in his two handicaps at around 1m3f when stamina was perhaps an issue

runners.name: Avec Grand Plaisir
runners.spotlight: Quiet in 1m-1m2f maidens but handicaps are now a better option, and it will be interesting to see what the market makes of him.
runners.comment: Quiet in 1m-1m2f maidens but handicaps are now a better option; market useful

runners.name: Ochtar Againn
runners.spotlight: His fifth on handicap debut at Gowran (1m, good to yielding) looked something to build on but she was very disappointing on her return visit only 13 days ago; in fairness, that was too bad to be true.
runners.comment: Disappointing 13 days ago but her previous handicap debut looked something to build on

runners.name: No More Hiding
runners.spotlight: Has failed to raise her modest profile in two handicaps at 1m, on the AW and soft to heavy; however, last run at Leopardstown wasn't too bad as she got a good way behind.
runners.comment: Last run at Leopardstown wasn't too bad as she got a good way behind

runners.name: Zheleznaya Dama
runners.spotlight: Much improved effort when beaten 2l into fifth at Down Royal in September and she was backed into favouritism; 3lb higher now; absent since, so interesting to see if her supporters return.
runners.comment: Improved effort when beaten 2l into fifth at Down Royal in September and she was backed

races.race_name: Adare Manor Opportunity Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Sound Judgement
runners.spotlight: Point winner and bumper-placed; pulled up on hurdling debut but was second to smart dual-purpose mare Galileo Dame over 2m at Navan; had an excuse when disappointing favourite last time; up in trip and bred to stay.
runners.comment: Second to smart dual-purpose mare Galileo Dame over 2m at Navan; had an excuse last time

runners.name: Lunar Majesty
runners.spotlight: Rated 96 after five runs in maidens at up to 2m4f; placed at Clonmel two runs back; more needed.
runners.comment: Rated 96 after five runs in maidens at up to 2m4f; placed at Clonmel two runs back

runners.name: Toor Idaho
runners.spotlight: Second over 2m6f at Ballinrobe last year and ran well over that trip at Thurles in November; totally out of form since.
runners.comment: Second over 2m6f at Ballinrobe and ran well at Thurles in November; poor form since

runners.name: Majestic Horizon
runners.spotlight: Won a useful contest on sole point start for Cormac Doyle and solid runs in two bumpers, including at this venue last time; half-brother Dark Raven was useful novice hurdler for Willie Mullins.
runners.comment: Point winner and solid runs in two bumpers, including at this venue last time

runners.name: Surfin Usa
runners.spotlight: Remote second when odds-on in Sligo bumper in 2024; out of action after that until well beaten when favourite at Down Royal in March; half-sister to 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Fun Fun Fun makes hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Well held in two bumpers; half-sister to smart jumper  Fun Fun Fun makes hurdling debut

runners.name: Open Approach
runners.spotlight: Berkshire gelding out of unraced sister to winners Adamantly Chosen (smart 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Uncle Pat (useful 2m6f hurdle/3m7f chase); only 5-1 for recent debut at Clonmel, carried out at the second but continued to finish in rear.
runners.comment: Only 5-1 for debut at Clonmel, carried out at the second but continued to finish in rear

runners.name: Nevermindestranger
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in a couple of handicaps last summer when with David Christie; was 100-1 for similar contest here last month; unlikely.
runners.comment: Ran okay in a couple of handicaps last summer; 100-1 for similar contest here last month

runners.name: Urra Mass Rock
runners.spotlight: Shirocco half-sister to bumper/2m5f hurdle winner The Boss Bear; dam unplaced in point/over hurdles, half-sister to dam of useful chaser Indian Temple, out of half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner Pollen.
runners.comment: Shirocco half-sister to bumper/2m5f hurdle winner The Boss Bear

runners.name: I Can Find Em
runners.spotlight: Court Cave gelding; runner-up twice in point-to-points in the season just ended, soundly beaten both times; 12l margin at Lingstown, 18l at Loughanmore; bought for E26,000 since; stable/rules debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice in point-to-points, bought for E26,000 since; stable/hurdling debut.

runners.name: Texas Red
runners.spotlight: Brother to an Italian Grade 2 2m1f hurdle winner; ran okay in bumpers last year when with Ronald O'Leary; off since September.
runners.comment: Brother to Italian Grade 2 hurdle winner; ran ok in bumper last year; off since September
runners.owner: John Henry & William Bates & Paul Hillis

runners.name: Gainsville
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three times in points for Colin Bowe; 12-1 for Clonmel 2m3f maiden hurdle in January, tailed off on deep ground.
runners.comment: Runner-up three times in points for; tailed off on deep ground on hurdling debut
runners.owner: Both Sides Of The Water Syndicate

runners.name: Acheron
runners.spotlight: 65-rated Flat maiden has finished tailed off on both completed hurdle runs.
runners.comment: 65-rated Flat maiden has finished tailed off on both completed hurdle runs

runners.name: Seekthisfairland
runners.spotlight: Big prices and heavy defeats in three maidens.
runners.comment: Big prices and heavy defeats in three maidens
runners.owner: Kevin Sexton & James McIlwee & James Convery & M G Byrne

runners.name: Tribal Moon
runners.spotlight: Did well on AW for Danny Murphy, two wins over 1m at Dundalk; second over 10.5f on stable debut when last seen in November; tailed off sole run over hurdles last summer.
runners.comment: Two AW wins over 1m, second over 10.5f on stable debut; tailed off sole hurdles outing

races.race_name: Randox Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: In The Trenches
runners.spotlight: Maiden has finished second 14 times and third six times across all codes; reverted from chasing to be second in Clonmel maiden hurdle last week.
runners.comment: Maiden has finished second 14 times across all codes, including in Clonmel hurdle latest

runners.name: Eye Contact
runners.spotlight: Had shown little, Flat or hurdles, before running way above expectations (300-1) in conditions hurdle at Ballinrobe; that run might be misleading as to his ability, but could be competitive at this level.
runners.comment: Ran way above expectations (300-1) in conditions hurdle at Ballinrobe; may be competitive

runners.name: Western Shores
runners.spotlight: Nice pedigree and showed promise when unfancied for recent Tramore debut, only beaten around 7l in fair 2m maiden won by 116-rated gelding, keeping on steadily; longer trip should suit.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree and showed promise when unfancied for Tramore debut

runners.name: Amina
runners.spotlight: Has run to a good level in four bumper starts well spread out; placed first three times, including a second over 2m3f, and fifth in Listed contest at Cheltenham when last seen in November; half-sister to Harry Fry's smart handicap hurdler Altobelli; yard quiet of late.
runners.comment: Has run to a good level in four bumper starts; half-sister to smart hurdler Altobelli

runners.name: Colwyn Bay
runners.spotlight: Outran 66-1 odds when third on debut in Clonmel bumper (2m3f, soft); took a backward step next time at Punchestown, for all it was a much tougher assignment; hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Outran 66-1 odds when third on debut in Clonmel bumper; took a backward step next time

runners.name: Euro Agent
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in a strong Leopardstown bumper for Eoin Griffin; well held in two maiden hurdle runs for this yard.
runners.comment: Ran ok in Leopardstown bumper for Eoin Griffin; held in two hurdle runs for this yard

runners.name: Magical Act
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred 5yo was 40s for bumper here last month, well beaten.
runners.comment: Flat-bred 5yo was 40s for bumper here last month, well beaten

runners.name: Double Order
runners.spotlight: First reserve; nice jumping pedigree but modest bumper form, including on first two starts for this yard last month.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree but modest bumper form, including on first two starts for yard; reserve

runners.name: Jet Renegade
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in points, bumpers and on the Flat (rated 66); well beaten on last month's hurdling debut at Aintree; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Has shown ability in points, bumpers and on the Flat;  well beaten on hurdling debut

runners.name: Sum Story
runners.spotlight: Sumbal gelding; dam unraced sister to bumper and 2m/2m1f chase winner Barranco, out of unraced half-sister to smart staying chaser Cannington Brook; unplaced in both points.
runners.comment: Sumbal gelding; dam unraced sister to bumper and 2m1f chase winner; unplaced in points
runners.owner: J J Leckey & Donal P Keane & P J Rothwell

runners.name: Adifferentballowax
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; Capri gelding made no impact in Punchestown festival bumper or Limerick maiden hurdle.
runners.comment: Capri gelding made no impact in Punchestown bumper or Limerick maiden hurdle; reserve
runners.owner: John F Doyle & R Bannerman & Mrs Sheila O'Ryan

runners.name: Allroadsleadtorome
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in good bumper at Naas on debut and when favourite for Kelso bumper next time; never a factor on hurdling debut at Kilbeggan a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Well held in two bumpers and on hurdling debut at Kilbeggan a fortnight ago

runners.name: Moundofthehostages
runners.spotlight: Diamond Boy gelding was tailed off in 2m4f bumper last month.
runners.comment: Diamond Boy gelding was tailed off in 2m4f bumper last month

runners.name: Yup Bro
runners.spotlight: Maiden after six Flat starts, rated 55; out the back on first run of the year at Dundalk; Belardo gelding not bred for this game; third runner for yard.
runners.comment: Maiden after six Flat starts, rated 55; Belardo gelding not bred for this game

runners.name: Paymelater
runners.spotlight: Placed twice and some other fair efforts in 10 bumper outings; first try over hurdles.
runners.comment: Placed twice and some other fair efforts in 10 bumper outings; first try over hurdles

runners.name: Mydreamtell
runners.spotlight: Sent off 80-1 at Ballinrobe, showed ability when sixth of 15 in 2m maiden hurdle.
runners.comment: Sent off 80-1 at Ballinrobe, showed ability when sixth of 15 in 2m maiden hurdle

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: She's A Fine Wine
runners.spotlight: Best right-handed and on good ground; both hurdle wins have been after a layoff; two wins over fences last year, over 2m4f at Kilbeggan and when easily landing Cork handicap over 2m1f in October; chase mark is now 130.
runners.comment: Both hurdle wins have been after a layoff; chase mark now 130 after easy Cork win

runners.name: You Make Me Smile
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, two on the Flat, two over hurdles; placed twice on the level this year and good run from well out of the handicap in 2m Ballinrobe hurdle last time; this trip might just stretch her stamina.
runners.comment: Good run from well out of the handicap in 2m Ballinrobe hurdle; trip might stretch her
runners.owner: T & R Hennessy & A Fahy Partnership

runners.name: Rockstown Girl
runners.spotlight: Tough sort gained third hurdles win in March when scoring over 2m4f at Down Royal; placed twice over fences since; first visit to this track.
runners.comment: Third hurdles win when landing 2m4f handicap at Down Royal; placed over fences since

runners.name: Wild Mandate
runners.spotlight: Placed in four bumpers and second to Tir Og at Sligo (extended 2m1f) on handicap hurdle debut; probably didn't stay 2m5f when last seen eight months ago; likes good ground.
runners.comment: Second to Tir Og at Sligo (extended 2m1f) on handicap hurdle debut; off eight months

runners.name: Miss Lia
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner has been second over longer trips in four of her last six starts in handicap hurdles, including over 2m6f here in March; sixth over fences here yesterday.
runners.comment: Second in four of her last six hurdle starts; sixth over fences here yesterday
runners.owner: Sussex By The Sea Syndicate/David L'Estrange

runners.name: Long Gone
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper and two hurdles for Declan Queally; unfancied but ran okay at Kilbeggan on first start of the year and second start for yard.
runners.comment: Won a bumper and two hurdles for Declan Queally; ran ok at Kilbeggan second start for yard

runners.name: Miss Manzor
runners.spotlight: Useful juvenile hurdler for Willie Mullins but didn't take to fences; beaten 20l in similar contest at Ballinrobe last month on second start for yard.
runners.comment: Useful juvenile hurdler for Willie Mullins but didn't take to fences; beaten 20l latest

runners.name: Radar Ahead
runners.spotlight: Won at Sligo on handicap debut and ran quite well at Navan afterwards off 109; placed in 1m4f/1m5f handicaps this year; in-form rider aboard.
runners.comment: Won at Sligo on handicap debut last summer; placed in 1m4f/1m5f handicaps this year

runners.name: Gangster Granny
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner added a first hurdling success when successful at 33-1 over 2m6f here last month; couldn't follow up in a similar contest here last time when a 20l fifth; 2nd over 2m6f here yesterday.
runners.comment: Won at 33-1 over 2m6f here; couldn't follow up in similar contest; 2nd here yesterday.

runners.name: Midnight Eagle
runners.spotlight: Winner at Limerick over a year ago and second at Thurles in November; acquired from a Clonmel claimer where she kept on for third over 2m3f; got 7lb for that.
runners.comment: Winner at Limerick over a year ago and second at Thurles in November; stable debut

runners.name: Hillhead Runner
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner for Peter Fahey and placed three times, once here, in summer maiden hurdles last year for Gavin Cromwell; after a long layoff, well beaten on stable debut at Cork; first run in a handicap.
runners.comment: Bumper winner and placed three times, once here, in summer maiden hurdles last year

runners.name: Tir Og
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well in the early part of last season, rising 30lb in the ratings, stemming from three wins and two seconds; well beaten lately and mark is starting to edge back down; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Progressed well in early part of last season, rising 30lb in ratings; well held lately

runners.name: Layenda
runners.spotlight: Had shown little before 200-1 remote third to facile winner in C&D maiden last month; goes handicapping now.
runners.comment: Had shown little before 200-1 remote third to facile winner in C&D maiden; first handicap

runners.name: Hasten Slowly
runners.spotlight: Last win came at Tramore in August but has lost form and struggling in claimers lately; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Last win came at Tramore in August but has lost form and struggling in claimers lately

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Arch Empire
runners.spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden hurdle winner last August; shaped with promise at big odds in hot handicap at Punchestown on penultimate and 4lb lower now but needs to bounce back from poor novice hurdle effort at Wexford 18 days ago.
runners.comment: Promise on penultimate at Punchestown, needs to bounce back from poor run latest

runners.name: Chanceawetmorning
runners.spotlight: Progressive for Gordon Elliott, winning three of four starts; easy novice hurdle win in three-runner race at Roscommon latest; now 15lb higher than last handicap victory and was beaten off 8lb lower at Limerick on penultimate; needs career best.
runners.comment: Won 3 of last 4, easy novice win latest, 15lb above last h'cap win, needs career best

runners.name: Metamorpheus
runners.spotlight: 1-18 over hurdles; went close to adding to that tally on latest at Kilbeggan when 0.75l behind Malbay Madness; 3lb better off now and rider also claims handy 3lb; could turn that around now and has gone well over this C&D before; place chance at least.
runners.comment: 3lb better off for 0.75l defeat to Malbay Madness latest, place chance at least

runners.name: Mr Master Mac
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; off mark at second attempt over hurdles for this rider in 2m3f maiden here last month (good); that form has taken a couple of knocks but can't be discounted dropped slightly in trip for handicap debut.
runners.comment: 2m3f maiden hurdle win here latest, drops in trip for handicap debut, can't discount

runners.name: Kamino Bello
runners.spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner last May; only seen thrice since and has failed to fire, including when beaten 44l on handicap debut at Punchestown latest; may have needed that, but others preferred for now.
runners.comment: Poor since Sligo maiden hurdle win, beaten 44l on h'cap bow latest, others preferred

runners.name: Cloonainra
runners.spotlight: Lost way since Listowel novice chase win in September; soundly beaten in Punchestown handicap hurdle on latest and just 2lb lower now; others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in Punchestown handicap latest, eased 2lb, others in better form

runners.name: Yosemite Gold
runners.spotlight: Roscommon maiden hurdle winner last August; soundly beaten in handicaps since and poor efforts on the AW in 2026; failed to beat a rival in 1m Dundalk claimer latest; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner, no impact in h'caps, poor on Flat latest, others appeal more

runners.name: Red Glory
runners.spotlight: Gained third win over timber by a nose when last seen at Galway in October (2m, gd-yld); raised 6lb for that; will need to be ready to rock after 231-day break off career high mark (has won when fresh before).
runners.comment: Up 6lb to career high mark for Galway in in Oct', off since, has gone well fresh before

runners.name: Malbay Madness
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; three-time hurdles winner, all three wins coming at Kilbeggan at around 2m on decent ground; 0.75l win there on latest (Metamorpheus second); may not uphold that form off 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Third Kilbeggan win on latest, up 6lb, may not uphold form with Metamorpheus

runners.name: Bridgehead
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner in July 2024; very modest efforts on Flat and back over hurdles on latest after long layoff; a bit to prove for now.
runners.comment: Modest efforts on Flat and over hurdles on latest after long layoff, a bit to prove now

runners.name: Dontgowithjoe
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick after successive wins at Ballinrobe and Bellewstown, both on good ground last August; has gone up a total of 13lb for those two wins, moves out of 0-100 company now and lacks a run but needs checking in the market.
runners.comment: Back-to-back 0-100 wins at Bellewstown last Aug', up 13lb and in grade, check market

races.race_name: Dunlop Homes Rated Novice Hurdle

runners.name: Butch Coolidge
runners.spotlight: Left previous ordinary form behind when winning a Tramore maiden hurdle 15 days ago (2m6f, good) in first-time hood (retained); handed a rating of 102 for that; raider claims 7lb but whether this sharper test will suit is debatable.
runners.comment: Drops in trip after 2m6f maiden win latest, rider claims 7lb, not sure this test will suit

runners.name: Lumiere Du Large
runners.spotlight: Maiden rated 105 after eight fruitless runs over hurdles; highly tried at times but beaten 11l and 20l on first two starts for this yard in maiden hurdles when second at Sligo (2m2f, good) and third at Wexford (2m4f, gd-yld); place shout again.
runners.comment: 0-8, rated 105, beaten 11l and 21l on first two runs for this yard, place shout

runners.name: Fedneys Park
runners.spotlight: In good hands but yet to show winning potential over hurdles; beaten 35l off 100 on most recent start in Wexford handicap (2m1f, gd-yld); can race keenly; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Yet to show winning potential, beaten 35l in handicap latest, others appeal more

runners.name: Sea Of Doubt
runners.spotlight: Goes well at this venue and won a C&D handicap off 92 on her penultimate start (2m2f, good); failed to back that up off 7lb higher mark over 2m3f on latest; place shout if she can get back on track.
runners.comment: Goes well here, C&D h'cap win on penultimate, place chance if repeats that

runners.name: Blue Moon Boy
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner; stand-out effort over hurdles when a 2.25l second of 15 off 93 at Sligo (2m2f, good) on penultimate; didn't build on that at Tramore on latest; capable of better but risks attached.
runners.comment: Flat winner, solid run on penultimate, didn't build on it latest, risks attached

runners.name: Pure Logic
runners.spotlight: Very solid bumper form; placed in a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle last April but well below that level in two runs over timber this year; cheekpieces tried at Listowel last time retained; needs to revive.
runners.comment: Solid placed bumper form, below best over hurdles last twice, needs a revival

runners.name: Maxwell Smart
runners.spotlight: Glimmers of promise in maiden hurdles last year and rated 102; decent effort off lowly mark on the Flat at Navan on latest (1m5f, good); rider takes off useful 7lb and would have a place chance on best form over timber.
runners.comment: Rated 102, decent Flat run off lowly mark latest, place chance on best hurdles form
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd'/D O'Leary/J McNally

runners.name: Beat The Devil
runners.spotlight: Flat AW winner was pulled up on sole start over hurdles in November; soundly beaten on return from a break on the Flat latest; hood added to tongue tie.
runners.comment: AW winner, soundly beaten first run over hurdles, poor on Flat latest, new headgear combo

runners.name: Annie's Angel
runners.spotlight: Flat winner on testing ground; 0-3 over hurdles, all three starts coming on heavy ground; not a bad run on second start in this sphere in a Hereford maiden (2m); rain would increase her chance.
runners.comment: Best form on Flat and over hurdles on heavy ground, rain would boost claims
runners.owner: Conor O'Connor & Colum Currie & Mark Devenny

runners.name: Houndsworth
runners.spotlight: Shock win when bolting up at 40-1 off 93 at Cork in April (2m, yielding); midfield off revised mark at Limerick later that month, but testing ground may not have suited; could resume progress returned to a sounder surface.
runners.comment: Bolted up at Cork on penultimate, ground possible excuse latest, could resume progress

races.race_name: Cab Plant Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Oh So Charming
runners.spotlight: Mostly disappointing since winning off 14lb higher at Clonmel last October; better effort in claiming hurdle at Wexford latest; more on plate now back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Step in right direction in Wexford claimer latest, more needed back in a handicap

runners.name: Demanding Gerry
runners.spotlight: Has finished runner-up in four of last five starts, including on return from 225 days off at Wexford on latest; nudged up another 1lb, but has gone well over C&D before and a leading contender.
runners.comment: Second in four of last five (inc' over C&D), sharper for latest, leading contender

runners.name: Magnificent Era
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 0-4 and heavy defeat on handicap debut at Clonmel just 9 days ago; still early days but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Heavy defeat on h'cap debut latest, still early days but others appeal more; reserve

runners.name: Bowgate Street
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win at Sligo (2m2f, good) on penultimate; raised 7lb and soundly beaten off revised mark at Ballinrobe; eased 1lb; may be on tough mark for now.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten off revised rating after Sligo win, eased 1lb, on tough mark for now

runners.name: Spanish John
runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner; 1-18 over hurdles but that win came in August 2023; remains 1lb above that sole success and ordinary effort on the level on return from break on latest; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 1-18 over timber, sole hurdles win in Aug' 2023, poor on Flat after break on latest

runners.name: Centaq
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles but runner-up on three occasions; didn't quite see out 2m3f when a 5.5l fourth of 10 here on latest; second over C&D on successive starts in the autumn; big player.
runners.comment: Runner up twice over C&D in autumn, 2m3f stretched here latest, player down in trip

runners.name: High Notions
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner last October at Fairyhouse; best effort yet in handicaps a 5.5l third of 13 at Kilbeggan two weeks ago; place shout again off same mark.
runners.comment: Much improved effort at Kilbeggan two weeks ago, place shout again off same mark
runners.owner: I'm Going To No Home Syndicate

runners.name: Bryson
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens and again when keeping on for sixth at Naas in December (2m, sft-hvy); unseated after a mistake at the fifth on return from a break at Wexford (2m1f, gd-yld) 18 days ago when just 5-1; can't discount.
runners.comment: 5-1 when unseating on return from break at Wexford on latest, has ability, can't discount
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & Mark Devlin & Philip Smith

runners.name: Walk Win
runners.spotlight: Looked limited before much better effort when a 1.75l runner-up on second handicap start at Kilbeggan two weeks ago (3.75l ahead of High Notions); 2lb higher and longer trip here a query, but relatively unexposed and big player now.
runners.comment: Much better effort latest, up 2lb and in trip, relatively unexposed, big player

runners.name: Boston Queen
runners.spotlight: Yet to show winning potential in five starts; near the back when brought down two out on latest here (2m5f, gd-yld) when 25-1; drops back in trip.
runners.comment: Yet to display winning potential, near the back when brought down here on latest

runners.name: Georginas Jet
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles but has run well in defeat on occasion; runner up over C&D in August off 2lb higher so not discounted but needs to rebound from two poor efforts since returning from a 197-day break.
runners.comment: Runner up off 2lb higher over C&D in Aug, poor last twice, needs to rebound

runners.name: Sonny Fella
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 0-14 and yet to trouble the judge; down the field again at Sligo on latest; hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: 0-14, yet to trouble judge, soundly beaten again on latest, hard to fancy; reserve

runners.name: The Poet's Way
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante hard to fancy on exploits in four maidens at 18.5f-2m6f on varying ground.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, hard to fancy on exploits in quartet of maiden hurdles

runners.name: Victoria Kesia
runners.spotlight: Flat winner didn't show much in four maiden hurdle runs; best form on the level on testing ground; likely best watched on handicap hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Flat winner, poor maiden hurdle form, likely best watched on handicap debut, likes it soft

runners.name: Dark Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Very ordinary form in maidens and no sign of progress so far in handicaps; beaten 22l at Sligo when last sighted in November; 2lb drop not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Ordinary form so far, beaten 22l at Sligo when last seen, 2lb drop not enough to tempt

runners.name: Copper Rocker
runners.spotlight: No show in maidens and was beaten when falling on handicap bow at Down Royal when last seen in January; new yard now and probably best watched after break.
runners.comment: Beaten when falling on h'cap bow latest, new yard, watch after break

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Magical Sal
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in two bumpers last autumn; more like it when a staying-on fifth of 9 over this C&D (good) on latest in first-time cheekpieces (retained); place claims if backing up that run.
runners.comment: Much-improved effort over C&D latest, place claims if backing that run up

runners.name: Churchfield Annie
runners.spotlight: Fine efforts in bumpers at Tipperary and Galway (both 2m, gd-yld) in first two starts; the form of that second run has worked out and heavy ground a possible excuse for poor run in a Listed bumper at Navan in November (also ran too freely); off since; leading form claims if fit; tongue tie on.
runners.comment: Fine efforts first two runs, ground possible excuse latest, tongue tied, leading chance

runners.name: Castle May
runners.spotlight: Conduit mare; dam fourth in bumper only start, half-sister to winners All The Eights (2m4f hurdle) and Miss Dimples (2m5f chase); needs checking in the market on debut.
runners.comment: Conduit mare, dam fourth in bumper, needs checking in the market on debut

runners.name: Irish Lace
runners.spotlight: 4yo Crystal Ocean filly; E4,000 yearling; dam unraced sister to winners The Happy Chappy (3m1f-3m4f hurdle/chase) and The Reaping Race (3m hurdle), out of bumper winner; yard's runners always respected in this sphere; check market.
runners.comment: 4yo Crystal Ocean filly, dam out of bumper winner, yard has success in this sphere

runners.name: Jet To Glory
runners.spotlight: Placed in four-runner point-to-point in March; beaten 33l when fifth of 24 in 2m Punchestown maiden hurdle on rules debut 8 days ago, staying on; longer trip will suit but more needed.
runners.comment: Stayed on for remote fifth over 2m in Punchestown maiden hurdle latest, more needed
runners.owner: Claire Wylie & Mrs Orla Cleary-O'Kane & Mark McGuckin

runners.name: Goodgollymissholly
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and pulled up in a Sligo mares' bumper last June; not seen since; has joined top yard so worth a market check, but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Sligo last June, off since, new yard, hard to fancy but check market

runners.name: Our Sara
runners.spotlight: Shaped with a bit of promise on second start at Bellewstown (2m1f, yielding), doing best work at the finish; disappointed when sent off at 13-2 at Wexford on latest; tongue-tie tried now; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Solid effort at Bellewstown on penultimate, didn't back it up latest, now tongue tied
runners.owner: Mrs Anne Gleeson/Miss Heather Heffernan/Gerard P O'Neill

runners.name: Freeway
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and ran accordingly in a trio of bumpers, including over this C&D the last twice; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Huge odds and no show in first three starts, seems safe to rule out

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence - Whips To Be Carried But Not Used)

runners.name: Allegrino
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when winning 7f AW maiden in January and again ran well when dropped back to 6f for handicap debut two weeks later; makes turf debut after four-month break but remains unexposed over this trip.
runners.comment: Won 7f AW maiden in January; second over 6f on handicap debut; back over 7f for turf debut

runners.name: Ahead Of Fashion
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 12 runs and his tendency to race too freely remains a worry but he returned to turf with a respectable fourth at Goodwood (7f, good to soft; wore cheekpieces) last week and this race is easier.
runners.comment: Returned to turf with respectable fourth at Goodwood last week; this race is easier

runners.name: Orange Emperor
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who tends to race too freely; close second of five on seasonal/handicap debut at Kempton (6f, AW) in March but two lesser efforts have followed (7f/6f), and he comes here with something to prove.
runners.comment: Close second on seasonal/handicap debut but two lesser efforts have followed

runners.name: Creciente
runners.spotlight: Last of 13 in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Lingfield (6f, good) last month and now 0-8; will be fine over this extra furlong but remains a difficult filly to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Last of 13 at Lingfield last month and now 0-8; difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Red Moon
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a significant blow in his three qualifying races (6f-7f) but has more realistic chance to get competitive in this run-of-the-mill handicap and is open to improvement.
runners.comment: Unable to land a significant blow in three qualifying races; may fare better in handicaps

races.race_name: Peter Lawrence Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Kisskodi
runners.spotlight: Record is 5-16; scored comfortably in similar event at Chepstow (7f, good to soft) last weekend, looking better than ever and effectively resuming the progress that he has shown sporadically; respected up 7lb.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably at Chepstow last weekend, resuming his progress; respected

runners.name: Diamondonthehill
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up but he has proved broadly consistent since last success, most recently beating all bar the market leader at Musselburgh (7f, good); again has place possibilities.
runners.comment: Consistent since last success, most recently second at Musselburgh; place claims

runners.name: Blue Prince
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since defying a 10lb higher mark at Glorious Goodwood two years ago but his two efforts this term are very encouraging, latest back at Goodwood (7f, soft); attractively treated and firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Won off 10lb higher at Goodwood two years ago; very encouraging efforts this term

runners.name: The Dragon King
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb higher in Class 4 on AW last November; has failed to land a blow in Class 3 races since returned to turf but this drop back in grade may have a positive effect; fairly useful in this sphere as a 2yo.
runners.comment: This drop back in grade may have a positive effect; 2lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Land Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Gradually back to form this term, most recently prevailing at Goodwood (6f, good to soft); has never won above Class 5 but she remains favourably treated, having defied current mark last term (over 7f).
runners.comment: Gradually back to form this term, most recently prevailing at Goodwood

runners.name: It'sneverjustone
runners.spotlight: Gained back-to-back wins, including on soft ground, last summer; couple of respectable fourth-place finishes this season, latest at Thirsk (7f, good); possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Couple of respectable efforts this term; possibilities if taking well to headgear
runners.owner: Ian O'Connor & The Fox And Hounds

runners.name: Spirit Of Breeze
runners.spotlight: Successful in Class 6 over C&D and Class 5 on AW last term; had wind surgery since final outing; needs to prove he can cope with this higher grade on reappearance but market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: C&D winner but has something to prove in this grade on reappearance

runners.name: Ararat
runners.spotlight: Completed an AW double last autumn but not in the same form since, latterly back on turf for new stable; hasn't scored in this sphere since 2023 and can be opposed.
runners.comment: Completed an AW double last autumn; not in the same form since

races.race_name: R Hannon Racing Supports The Savitri Waney Charity EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Sakowin
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on either start (6f/7f) but sets only a modest form standard and takes on some interesting newcomers.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on either start (6f/7f) but sets a only modest form standard

runners.name: Sonny Parvenue
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros breeze-up 2yo; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners In Paradise (French 1m1f/10.5f) and Roger (2m2f hurdle); entered in Irish Group 1/2 races this summer, and seemingly held in some regard.
runners.comment: 100,000euros breeze-up 2yo; holds Group-race entries; seemingly held in some regard

runners.name: Rising Tiger
runners.spotlight: 52,000gns yearling; ninth foal; half-brother to winners Themaxwecan (1m2f-2m including 2yo; RPR 107), Moai (1m/1m1f; 103), Mars Landing (1m-1m2f; 93) and a hurdle winner; dam Listed-placed 1m AW 2yo winner (92); in good hands; market may guide.
runners.comment: Out of a Listed-placed 2yo winner; in good hands; market may guide

runners.name: Weekend Roar
runners.spotlight: £105,000 yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Room Service (5.5f-6.5f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 115), Royal Advice (6f/7f; 80) and two European winners; dam placed 8.6f/1m1f (59); enters calculations on debut.
runners.comment: £105,000 yearling; half-brother to a Listed sprint winner; enters calculations

runners.name: Jumeirah Storm
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Tafreej (6f 2yo/1m) and Ubahha (5f); other newcomers are more enticing.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to a 2yo winner but other newcomers are more enticing

runners.name: Kigali
runners.spotlight: 12th foal; half-brother to six winners including Arctic Sound (7f-1m4f including 2yo Group 3; RPR 109), Breeze Easy (Grade 3-placed 5.5f/6f including 2yo/US/Canada; 102) and Deep Snow (1m/1m2f; 92); dam 5f and 6f (Listed) 2yo winner (94); no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Out of a 2yo Listed winner who has produced a 2yo Group 3 winner; interesting
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs B V Sangster

runners.name: Another Encore
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling; third foal; dam placed 1m2f/1m3f in France (including 2yo; RPR 71), sister to smart 1m1f/1m2f winner Collins Street; trainer has only occasional first-time-out 2yo winners.
runners.comment: Out of a sister to a smart middle-distance winner; stable not prolific with 2yo newcomers
runners.owner: Dean De Zoete Inglett Morris Palmer Rees

runners.name: Arctic Tsar
runners.spotlight: 90,000euros yearling; third foal; dam placed 1m4f (RPR 75), half-sister to four-time Australian Group 1 winner Zaaki (1m1f-1m2f) and two other winners; stable's 2yos almost always of interest.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to a four-time Australian Group 1 winner; with good yard; considered

runners.name: Gold Reef City
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Egoli (RPR 90); dam Listed-placed 1m winner (103), half-sister to winners Havana Gold (1m Group 1) and Rocker (multiple 5f including 2yo), out of dual 5f Group 3 winner; with a top stable; looks the part on paper.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt from a top stable; makes obvious appeal on paper

runners.name: Texas Crown
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful 6f winner Aplomb; would be a rare 2yo winner for his jumps-oriented stable.
runners.comment: Would be a rare 2yo winner for his jumps-oriented stable

runners.name: Theheatison
runners.spotlight: Out of a useful 2yo winner but was beaten about 18l when fifth of six on last month's debut at Lingfield (7f, good); drops in trip here.
runners.comment: Out of a useful 2yo winner but was beaten about 18l when fifth of six on debut (7f)

races.race_name: iwrightsongs.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Wild Violet
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 10l into third behind a smart filly at Newmarket (1m2f, good) and it was a useful novice in which she ran second at Wolverhampton (9.4f), still looking green; should have every chance.
runners.comment: Positives to take from both runs and still looked green last time at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Asset
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to six winners including three at Group level; one formline from her debut third at Kempton gives her similar claims to Wild Violet and probably ran better at Lingfield (1m2f, good), for all that she was turned over at odds of 1-2 in a small field; chance.
runners.comment: Probably ran better at Lingfield (1m2f), for all that she was turned over at odds of 1-2
runners.owner: Gestuet Schlenderhan Gmbh U Co Kg

runners.name: Brunhilde
runners.spotlight: Well related filly; slowly away at Southwell (1m; 18-1) but saw her race out on the front foot, just over 6l off the winner; should improve.
runners.comment: Slowly away at Southwell (1m; 18-1) but saw her race out on the front foot

runners.name: Queen Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; ninth foal; closely related to 6f/7f 2yo winner Isabella Giles (RPR 108), half-sister to three winners including Majestic Colt (German 6f-7.7f; Group 3; 109) and Novel Legend (14.3f-16.5f; 106); dam 5.7f and 6f (Group 3) 2yo winner (101); has to be feared on debut.
runners.comment: 500,000gns yearling; closely related to 6f/7f 2yo winner Isabella Giles (RPR 108)

runners.name: Perzaaneh
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Flat/hurdle winner Double Oban and another Flat winner; dam 7f/8.6f Flat winner (RPR 81); withdrawn from intended debut in a Wincanton bumper in November; hood applied.
runners.comment: Withdrawn from intended debut in a Wincanton bumper in November; hood applied
runners.owner: G E Bassett & P R Chamings

runners.name: Noble Grace
runners.spotlight: Second in a bumper but 0-6 over hurdles; well beaten in two Flat races and vulnerable once more.
runners.comment: Second in a bumper but 0-6 over hurdles; well beaten in two Flat races

runners.name: Oklahoma Dream
runners.spotlight: Ran to a modest level when fifth over 7f at Chelmsford in November when she was slowly away; best watched now she returns in a hood.
runners.comment: Ran to a modest level when fifth over 7f at Chelmsford in November; now hooded
runners.owner: D Macauliffe, A Don & G Rutter

races.race_name: H S Lester Memorial Challenge Cup 'Confined' Handicap (No Win Since 2025 Flat Turf Season)

runners.name: King's Castle
runners.spotlight: Gained back-to-back wins over 1m2f last summer; best effort since when close second on the turf course at Lingfield (11.5f) on reappearance, showing he retains ability aged nine; may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Close second on reappearance, showing he retains ability aged nine

runners.name: Huggable
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has form figures of 122 over 1m2f and may still have more to offer; kept on for midfield effort over 1m on seasonal debut; interesting back up in trip with that outing under her belt.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 122 over 1m2f; may still have more to offer; interesting

runners.name: Mighty Quiet
runners.spotlight: Returns to her optimum trip, having looked a non-stayer over 1m4f last time; likely player on the pick of her 1m2f form, namely two wins (2024/2025) and a creditable second (last autumn).
runners.comment: Returns to her optimum trip and is a likely player on best 1m2f form

runners.name: Suhub
runners.spotlight: Made the frame several times for previous yard, without progressing; however, ran encouragingly at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) on stable/seasonal debut and may build on that second-place finish; one to consider.
runners.comment: Maiden; ran encouragingly at Wolverhampton on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Primo Lara
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-7 for current yard but he went down by only a neck in first-time blinkers at Nottingham (1m2f, good) most recently; still well treated on historical data; possibilities in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Went down by only a neck in first-time blinkers at Nottingham most recently

runners.name: Criminal
runners.spotlight: Maiden who had a reasonably solid record last term but proved far less consistent this year; otherwise capable of taking a serious hand, provided he turns up in good form.
runners.comment: Maiden who has lost his consistency this year; best form gives him a shout

runners.name: Redbud Sixteen
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden whose form dipped in 1m AW contest on stable/handicap debut when last seen; 33-1 that day and the betting may again give some guidance.
runners.comment: Form dipped on stable/handicap debut when last seen; 33-1 that day

runners.name: Somkiyr
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 5yo who is far from solid on his British form (Flat/hurdles) and remains best watched unless the market suggests a resurgence is on the way.
runners.comment: Far from solid on his British form

runners.name: Castle Quarter
runners.spotlight: Only a modest hurdles winner for current yard and isn't convincing on his 2026 form in that sphere; not seen on Flat since 2021; others preferred.
runners.comment: Modest hurdles winner for current yard; not seen on Flat since 2021
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs R Newiss & Gavin Teversham

runners.name: Sixfiveseven
runners.spotlight: Gradually finding his form for new stable since returning from lengthy absence, fourth over 1m at Ffos Las most recently; doesn't seem to be crying out for this step back up in distance, however.
runners.comment: Doesn't seem to be crying out for this step back up in distance

races.race_name: Shipseys Marquees Handicap

runners.name: Eutropia
runners.spotlight: Three wins this year, the last two at Bath (1m/1m2f) in April; denied clear run before keeping on for fifth there (1m, good to firm)  last month and may yet have more improvement to come; up in grade here but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Three wins this year; denied clear run when fifth last time; may still be improving

runners.name: Kamaway
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who won by 3l on last month's turf debut at Yarmouth and ran well in defeat under a penalty there a week later; could still have more to offer; likely contender.
runners.comment: Won by 3l on last month's turf debut at Yarmouth and ran well there again a week later

runners.name: Rating
runners.spotlight: Placed three consecutive times at Bath before justifying favouritism in four-runner race there (1m, firm) 19 days ago; nudged up only 2lb for that but this race is much deeper; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Nudged up only 2lb for recent small-field win but this race is much stronger

runners.name: The Hare Rail
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 17 starts but scores good marks for consistency; runner-up in first-time visor (retained) at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last month and ought to be on the premises again here.
runners.comment: 0-17 but consistent; second in new visor at Windsor last month; in the mix again
runners.owner: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward

runners.name: Opening Bat
runners.spotlight: Scored on AW last spring and subsequently ran well over C&D; on good mark if judged on those exploits but ended last season with a below-par effort and was well beaten when returning last month; now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: On workable mark but ran poorly on seasonal debut and has a point to prove
runners.owner: Mrs R J McCreery And Richard Craddock

runners.name: Sant Alessio
