runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, the latter on Tapeta (8.6f) in November; also effective on turf but made a low-key reappearance last month and needs that run to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Won twice in 2025 but made a low-key return last month; needs to have come on for the run

runners.name: Norflondonforever
runners.spotlight: Came good at Kempton (1m, AW; first-time tongue-tie) in April 2025, his fifth start, but wasn't seen again until heavy defeat at Haydock (1m2f, good to soft) last month; drops back in trip here; tricky to weigh up.
runners.comment: AW winner in spring 2025 but absent until heavy defeat on turf last month

runners.name: Thiscouldbefun
runners.spotlight: Placed three consecutive times on turf (1m, good to form-heavy) in refitted cheekpieces in September and won on Polytrack (1m) the following month; made satisfactory reappearance ten days ago (no headgear) and is of interest with cheekpices back on again here.
runners.comment: Made satisfactory reappearance ten days ago and has cheekpieces refitted here; considered
runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

runners.name: Spirit Of The Bay
runners.spotlight: Gained last season's win over 1m2f and made an encouraging reappearance when finishing strongly for close fourth over 7f at Chepstow (good to soft) last month; sure to be suited by this move back up in trip and remains on a workable mark; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Flashed home over 7f on seasonal debut; will be suited by this return to 1m; shortlisted

runners.name: Lunanova
runners.spotlight: Won three times for two different stables at Brighton last year (1m, good to firm-good) but was soundly beaten on first three starts for current stable this spring (all 7f); considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten all three stable starts; considered only if lively in the betting

runners.name: Dark Rosa
runners.spotlight: Now only 1lb higher than for emphatic AW win in November but needs to up her game after three underwhelming turf runs this spring.
runners.comment: Needs to up her game after three underwhelming turf runs this spring

runners.name: Darvel
runners.spotlight: Did well last year (three wins; one over C&D) but is yet to strike form after three starts this season and slow starts have become a serious issue; goes without usual blinkers today; comes with risk.
runners.comment: Yet to strike form this season and slow starts have become a serious issue

runners.name: Spirit Lead Me
runners.spotlight: 4-14 on AW but 0-10 on turf; badly out of form this year (six runs) and needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Badly out of form this year (six runs) and needs to turn a corner

races.race_name: Molson Coors Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Taritino
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over hurdles this year and only went down by a head on his return to Flat action last week at Bath (1m6f, good); effective over this shorter trip and looks a good bet to give his running, which can't be said for all of these.
runners.comment: Looks a good bet to give his running, which can't be said for all of these

runners.name: Johnny Boom
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner with soft-ground form and arrives here on the back of two competitive efforts over 1m4f; solid claims.
runners.comment: Multiple winner with soft-ground form and arrives here after two competitive efforts

runners.name: Just An Hour
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who seems fine on any ground and wouldn't be far away with a repeat of his second at Nottingham (1m6f, good), though he didn't appear to run to the same level here a month ago.
runners.comment: Wouldn't be far away with a repeat of his second at Nottingham two runs back

runners.name: Fair Dinkum
runners.spotlight: Perhaps better over further and when going close over C&D a month ago he was edged out by a 66-1 chance who wasn't in much form before that race and has disappointed since; may need a tad more here, though Taryn Langley takes off a handy 5lb.
runners.comment: When going close over C&D a month ago he was edged out by a 66-1 chance

runners.name: Twilight Moon
runners.spotlight: Has done all her winning at Class 5 level and seemed to get found out off this mark upped to a Class 4 at Lingfield (1m4f AW); she will at least handle conditions, even if they dry out a bit.
runners.comment: Seemed to get found out off this mark upped to a Class 4 at Lingfield

runners.name: Arcturus Flame
runners.spotlight: Solid enough run behind the favourite last time at Goodwood (1m6f, good) but he's 7lb higher than for his only handicap win and there are doubts about him on soft ground.
runners.comment: 7lb higher than for his only handicap win and there are doubts about him on soft ground

runners.name: Tryfan
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner and also successful on good to firm; has disappointed three times on soft ground so not sure about him staging a revival on this occasion, no matter what effect cheekpieces have.
runners.comment: Disappointed three times on soft so not sure about him staging a revival on this occasion

races.race_name: Apres Christmas Village At Doncaster Racecourse Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Holly Mist
runners.spotlight: Only had the seven races and could improve for going this far judged on her keeping-on fifth over 1m2f at Pontefract; was beaten only 2.5l that day.
runners.comment: Could improve for going this far judged on her keeping-on fifth over 1m2f at Pontefract

runners.name: Idyllic
runners.spotlight: Has won over 1m2f, 1m3f and 1m4f; however, she closed out last season under a cloud and was again below her best on reappearance despite having the run of things up front; had a wind operation in April.
runners.comment: Closed out last season under a cloud and again below her best on reappearance

runners.name: Tiger
runners.spotlight: 1m1f winner in France last March; beaten about 4l in 1m/1m4f races in recent weeks so seems to be finding his feet for new yard and this is a drop in grade; fast ground is an unknown.
runners.comment: Won in France; seems to be finding his feet for new yard and this is a drop in grade

runners.name: Red Derek
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has won only twice in 56 career starts and this season's fourths were remote; 2lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Won only twice in 56 career starts and this season's fourths were remote; 2lb wrong

runners.name: Caledonian
runners.spotlight: Won a nursery off 4lb higher in 2024; below that level when confined to only three appearances last year and while his reappearance wasn't devoid of promise, his run at Newmarket was; goes beyond 1m2f for the first time.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on at all since winning a nursery during his opening campaign

runners.name: Optician
runners.spotlight: 5-19 on AW but 0-15 on the grass after finishing well behind at Thirsk two weeks ago (1m4f, good); he'll be of greater interest when back on the synthetics.
runners.comment: 5-19 on AW but 0-15 on the grass after finishing well behind at Thirsk two weeks ago

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Venetian Sky
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Newmarket on her sole start as a 2yo; better form in two fillies' novice events this year but others have achieved more and she will need the new headgear to give her a lift.
runners.comment: Two fair efforts this year but she will need more for the addition of headgear

runners.name: Quisana
runners.spotlight: Ran to a fair level in two maidens last month (1m, good and 7f, good to firm), finishing a head second at Chepstow 16 days ago; should give her running again but might find one or two too strong.
runners.comment: Fair form in two maiden runs last month; each-way claims once again

runners.name: Raneem
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam Listed-placed 7.5f 2yo winner (RPR 100), half-sister to winners Tamayuz (1m Group 1) and Nuqoosh (Group-placed 7f 2yo), out of 1m winning half-sister to French Derby winner Anabaa Blue; represents a powerful stable and the betting should be a good guide.
runners.comment: Dam a well-connected winner; represents a powerful yard and a market check is essential

runners.name: Enchant
runners.spotlight: £300,000 breeze-up purchase; only had two rivals to beat when winning a Pontefract fillies' novice (6f, good) 357 days ago but she overcame inexperience to win a shade cosily; penalty demands more but she should have progress in her this year.
runners.comment: Overcame inexperience to win at Pontefract last June (6f, good); open to improvement

runners.name: Bella Angelina
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to 7f 2yo AW winner Brave Mission (RPR 96); dam 6f 2yo winner (98), half-sister to winners Juliet Foxtrot (US 8.5f Grade 1), Juliet Sierra (6f 2yo Group 3), Tsar (useful 1m) and Cartago (1m 2yo); had a wind op in January; hooded for debut; enough paper appeal to come into the reckoning.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 7f winner out of winning and well-related dam; had wind op; hooded on debut

runners.name: Dream Vega
runners.spotlight: Has a fine pedigree and she made a satisfactory start to her career when fourth of seven, beaten 3l, in a 7f novice at Salisbury six weeks ago; that form looks solid and she is open to plenty of improvement; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Fine pedigree and last month's Salisbury debut was promising; plenty more to come
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill

runners.name: Shamalza
runners.spotlight: Well backed when winning a Navan fillies' maiden on her debut last September (1m, good); flopped on heavy going on her only other start last year and sold out of Dermot Weld's stable for 190,000euros in February; likely there is more in the tank and drying conditions will help.
runners.comment: Flopped on heavy latest but debut win was promising; sold for 190,000euros in February

runners.name: Able Astra
runners.spotlight: No better than midfield in two 7f novice runs so far; can do better at some point but she faces a stiff task today.
runners.comment: No better than midfield in two 7f novice runs so far; a big step forward is essential

runners.name: Crown Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two fillies' maidens over further this spring; no appeal.
runners.comment: Modest form in two fillies' maidens over further this spring; no appeal

runners.name: Arc La Shari
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in two AW runs in late 2025 but still an unlikely winner in today's field.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in two AW runs in late 2025 but still an unlikely winner in today's field
runners.owner: Ms C Dolan & Ms Sarah Harrison-Lamb

runners.name: Lady Vega
runners.spotlight: Nice pedigree and she attracted some support prior to finishing fourth of eight in a 6f maiden at Pontefract 23 days ago (good to soft); that form needs improving upon but 7f will suit and she's capable of better.
runners.comment: Promise on last month's Pontefract debut (6f); 7f should suit; open to improvement

runners.name: Sweet Desire
runners.spotlight: Her second run was a good step forward from her debut effort (both 6f, good) but she's unlikely to come into her own until tackling handicaps.
runners.comment: Second run more promising than her debut; handicaps more suitable after this

runners.name: Spruces Due
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a 6f AW novice 369 days ago when trained by Tjade Collier; can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a 6f AW novice 369 days ago when trained by Tjade Collier; can't recommend

races.race_name: Hilton Garden Inn Doncaster "Confined" Novice Stakes (Two Runs Or Less) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Nochebuena
runners.spotlight: Promise over 7f on her 2yo debut and again when second of six, beaten a neck, at Haydock 23 days ago (1m2f, soft; third-placed horse has won easily since); stepping up in trip can help and she's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on seasonal return at Haydock (1m2f, soft) last month; more to come upped in trip

runners.name: Kenneth
runners.spotlight: Bumped into a smart opponent when second on his Newbury debut last April (1m3f, good); absent 378 days (gelded) prior to an odds-on reverse at Southell seven weeks ago, a race where he looked a tricky ride; now tongue tied; remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Beaten fav, showing ability and waywardness, in two starts a year apart; now tongue tied

runners.name: White Storm
runners.spotlight: Best of the rest, beaten 11l, by a fine prospect on his Kempton debut (1m3f) 18 days ago; open to improvement; one to consider.
runners.comment: Best of the rest behind a runaway winner on Kempton debut 18 days ago (1m3f); more to come

runners.name: Eben Al Khawaneej
runners.spotlight: Beaten 11.5l and 14l in his two runs in middle-distance maidens this spring; should do better at some point.
runners.comment: Fair ability despite finishing well beaten in two middle-distance maidens this spring

runners.name: Jumpingthegun
runners.spotlight: Unfancied in two Irish maidens (1m, heavy and 9.5f, yielding) but he didn't run badly when fourth of 15 at Gowran on the second occasion; not bred to want this far but he remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Unfancied in two Irish maidens but he has shown ability; not bred to need this far

runners.name: Knight Of Storms
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and little short-term promise when tailed off at Newbury last month (1m2f, good).
runners.comment: 33-1 and little short-term promise when tailed off at Newbury last month (1m2f, good)

races.race_name: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Rousham
runners.spotlight: Prevailed by 1l at Redcar (7f, good to firm) last time on handicap debut; may build on that success and show further improvement at an ordinary level; respected.
runners.comment: Prevailed by 1l at Redcar last time on handicap debut; may improve further; respected

runners.name: Surgeon Commander
runners.spotlight: Still has something to prove on turf but returning to 7f may a have positive effect; 2-3 over this distance, both wins on Newcastle AW.
runners.comment: Returning to 7f may have positive effect; 2-3 over this trip, both wins at Newcastle

runners.name: Popty Ping
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW and overall record is just 1-19; however, turf form includes a respectable fourth over C&D in April; frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Raced mostly on AW but turf form includes a respectable C&D effort in April

runners.name: Norfolk Blue
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to a couple of fourth-place finishes over 1m this term, latest at Windsor; gives the impression he may benefit from this drop back in trip; possibilities.
runners.comment: Has encouraging form this term and dropping back to 7f may prove ideal

runners.name: Gunalt Wavelength
runners.spotlight: Has started her 2026 campaigned in good fettle (over 6f) and got off the mark in Ripon contest (good) last time; has form over 7f; likely to remain competitive.
runners.comment: Has started her 2026 season in good fettle; off the mark in Ripon contest last time

runners.name: Hakin Adraar
runners.spotlight: Ran encouragingly at Kempton (1m, AW) on stable/seasonal debut, while shaping as if this return to 7f will suit; could go well, provided he's effective back on turf.
runners.comment: AW reappearance effort suggests this drop back to 7f will suit; could go well

runners.name: Circle Of Trust
runners.spotlight: Best form when second on two occasions, including last time when 5l behind the winner in Thirsk maiden (7f); needs some progress to defy top weight.
runners.comment: 5l behind the winner in Thirsk maiden last time; needs progress to defy top weight
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Uncertainty
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times for previous yard but looks far from solid on her form for current stable; best watched unless the market suggests a resurgence is likely.
runners.comment: Far from solid on her form for current stable

runners.name: Forever Twenty
runners.spotlight: Has inconsistent form but ran well (1l third) over C&D on penultimate start and that form puts her in the picture; 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D on penultimate start; that form puts her in the picture

runners.name: Only Dream Big
runners.spotlight: Has performed encouragingly in her two starts this term and the latest effort (6f) suggests returning to 7f will prove ideal; first win is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Has performed encouragingly in her two starts this term; not ruled out

runners.name: Singarda
runners.spotlight: Seemed a non-stayer over 1m3f last time but it's questionable whether he needs a drop back to a distance as short as 7f; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Questionable whether he needs a drop back to a trip as short as 7f
runners.owner: D Bishop, S Bland & T Coyle

runners.name: Eyes Front
runners.spotlight: Has broadly stagnated this year and finished behind Norfolk Blue at Windsor (1m) most recently; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Chance largely depends on the effects of first-time blinkers

runners.name: Black Rock Beauty
runners.spotlight: Took well to first-time cheekpieces at Redcar (7f, good) in latest start, opening his account; may have more to offer in the retained headgear; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Took well to first-time cheekpieces at Redcar latest start, opening his account

runners.name: Willowinghurn
runners.spotlight: Proved somewhat resurgent in the Redcar event won by Rousham and is now 4lb below the mark she won off last October (7f, soft); looks a possible danger.
runners.comment: Proved somewhat resurgent last time and is on an attractive mark; possible danger

runners.name: Magic Lady Mae
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid on her AW form for Richard Hannon; sold for 4,800gns since last run; chance partly depends on the effects of switching to turf.
runners.comment: Chance partly depends on the effects of switching to turf; new yard
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Lifeguard
runners.spotlight: Has weak claims on her AW efforts for new yard and chance partly depends on the effects of a physical tweak (wind surgery since last run).
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of physical tweak (wind surgery)

races.race_name: Grace And Dotty Fedoras And Feathers Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Wrydcroft
runners.spotlight: Four wins (including C&D) and a second from his six handicap runs; still has time to progress further and he looks a solid candidate once more.
runners.comment: Building up a head of steam in 7f handicaps; another big run looks on the cards
runners.owner: G Libson And Mrs P Sly

runners.name: Tennessee Gold
runners.spotlight: Should be well handicapped but that has been the case for a while and his losing run stretches back 18 months; attracted support at Yarmouth last month (7f, good) but fared no better; now tried in a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Should be well treated but that has been the case for a while; now tried in a tongue-tie

runners.name: Highfield Viking
runners.spotlight: Gained last year's two wins over 7f on good ground in August and came second in a 15-runner race next time; Beverley reappearance was promising but he was only midfield in a big field at York three weeks ago;  down in class but needs a full revival.
runners.comment: Conditions no issue and drops in class; does need a full revival to take this though

runners.name: Tardaff
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in 7f fillies' novice and maiden events at Newmarket and Thirsk last summer; raced too freely when last of 13 at Newmarket on last month's return to action (1m, good); too much to prove for comfort.
runners.comment: Progress has stalled following early promise; too much to prove for comfort

runners.name: Beale Street
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner, the latest (May) his first over 7f; still seeking a first turf success but his two best runs on the grass came over C&D last summer; not discounted.
runners.comment: Still seeking a first turf win but his two best efforts on grass have come over C&D

runners.name: The Childe Of Hale
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a C&D handicap (good to soft) last July when rated 2lb higher; yet to shine in 2026 but the return to 7f and Doncaster could revive his fortunes.
runners.comment: C&D win last summer off 2lb higher; not fired yet in 2026 but revival not impossible
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Magic Boy
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a breakthrough win at Redcar (7f, good); similar form when second of ten at Ripon (6f, good) on his return to action 11 days ago; runs off the same mark today and he is one to take seriously back up to 7f.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; promising return (6f, good) 11 days ago; contender back up to 7f

runners.name: Green Pursuit
runners.spotlight: On a good mark on 2025 best and he has been running himself into form this time around, third of ten (beaten 1l) at Carlisle four weeks ago; conditions no problem and he is one to consider.
runners.comment: Running himself into form this year, close third at Carlisle latest; each-way shout again

runners.name: Mereside Madness
runners.spotlight: Well short of his best since joining this yard, only beating a handful of runners home in his four runs in 2026; tumbling down the weights but not a solid option.
runners.comment: Tumbling down the weights but with some justification; too much to prove at present

runners.name: Alpha Capture
runners.spotlight: Not the force of old but he retains ability and has performed with credit for his new yard this year, most recently finishing second of nine at Pontefract 12 days ago (6f, good); down in class today and should go well despite his small rise in the weights.
runners.comment: Solid efforts for new yard, second at Pontefract (6f) latest; up 2lb but down in class

runners.name: Caragio
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin turf win in April flanked by two AW defeats as favourite; perhaps the return to turf will help but he is creeping back up the weights.
runners.comment: Wide-margin turf win in April flanked by two defeats as favourite on AW; edging up weights

runners.name: Jenever
runners.spotlight: Multiple sprint winner, the latest off a 3lb higher mark last October (6f, good to soft); not at his best so far this year and 7f is a query.
runners.comment: Multiple sprint winner but not at his best this year and 7f is a query

races.race_name: Grace And Dotty Fedoras And Feathers Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Follow Your Heart
runners.spotlight: Both turf wins have come at Doncaster, the second in the corresponding event 12 months ago; wouldn't judge him too harshly on York last time and the return to this track and Class 5 company could see him rebound.
runners.comment: Both turf wins at Doncaster, including this race in 2025; return to form could be on cards

runners.name: Pulsar Star
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden who finished second at Kempton (7f, AW) and Nottingham (8.3f, good; handicap debut) on his first two runs this year; midfield finish at York three weeks ago; respected back down in class.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; drops back into a Class 5 and still has a bigger effort in him

runners.name: Berry Clever
runners.spotlight: He had been banging at the door prior to his Yarmouth win last month (7f, good); similar form when a close fourth back at Yarmouth one week later; not ruled out.
runners.comment: In good form this year, including a 7f win at Yarmouth; should remain competitive

runners.name: Ziggy's Condor
runners.spotlight: All wins for former yard came over 6f but he does stay this longer trip; didn't run badly on last month's stable debut but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: All wins over 6f but does stay this trip; fair stable debut last month; others appeal more

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: 12lb lower than for his win in the Great St Wilfrid last August (6f, good to firm); mixed bag in 2026 but he is the type to pop up soon, albeit probably over a shorter trip.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but 2026 has been a mixed bag; this perhaps not his day

runners.name: Macedonian
runners.spotlight: AW scorer for previous yard; inconsistent for current stable and has failed to show his form in two starts this term; well treated on best turf efforts but he's not particularly solid.
runners.comment: Inconsistent for Michael Dods and not shone so far in 2026; others appeal more

runners.name: Commander Of Life
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; despite the stable's poor year he has run a string of fine races in 2026, including when a close fifth of 22 in a Class 4 at York three weeks ago; shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: C&D winner; string of good runs in defeat this year and another bold bid seems likely

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: The winner of two 7f AW handicaps for Scott Dixon; didn't run badly on turf at Lingfield (7f, good) on her penultimate start and her rider takes off a useful 3lb; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Two 7f AW wins for new yard; effective on turf too; each-way shout

runners.name: They All Know Me
runners.spotlight: Not straightforward but plenty of ability for one plying his trade at Class 5 level; off the mark at the ninth attempt when winning a Carlisle handicap four weeks ago (7f, soft); 2lb rise not beyond him if things pan out favourably once again.
runners.comment: 2lb rise for Carlisle win not beyond him but he will need the cards to fall just right

runners.name: Shazani
runners.spotlight: Made all in the Leger Legends race here last September (1m, good to soft); yet to shine this season but he's becoming dangerously well treated.
runners.comment: 1m win here last September; not hit the same heights this time around

runners.name: Chifa
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Wolverhampton over the winter (7f); well beaten over C&D on his return to turf six weeks ago; something to prove after that.
runners.comment: Well beaten over C&D six weeks ago on his return to turf; opposable on the back of that

runners.name: Mystical Storm
runners.spotlight: 6f novice win here last summer came after a wind op; well beaten at Pontefract on his seasonal return, after which he underwent further wind surgery; dangerous mark if it has the desired effect.
runners.comment: Won here after wind op last summer (6f) and he now bids to repeat the feat; good mark

races.race_name: Royal Ascot Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Mini Mac
runners.spotlight: Back in better form over 7f at Doncaster and Leicester the last twice; still attractively treated, having gained sole handicap win off 12lb higher (over 1m); respected.
runners.comment: Back in better form the last twice; remains attractively treated; respected

runners.name: Ey Up He's A Star
runners.spotlight: Gained sole win on AW; inconsistent kept to 7f this term but has possibilities off current mark provided he takes well to first-time cheekpieces and new trip.
runners.comment: Possibilities off current mark provided he takes well to cheekpieces and new trip

runners.name: Onemorenomore
runners.spotlight: Has gained all wins at shorter, including three times off higher marks at Doncaster; however, not disgraced over 8.5f last time and enters calculations back here.
runners.comment: Three-time winner off higher marks at Doncaster; enters calculations back here

runners.name: Evelyn's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Good start to the campaign featured a win but he's not been in the same form since; it doesn't look like this will be his day.
runners.comment: Good start to the campaign but he's not been in the same form since

runners.name: Maui Breeze
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW; not solid on her form for current stable and a return to turf had no effect last time.
runners.comment: Not solid on her form for current stable
runners.owner: H S Hutchinson And W Glass

runners.name: Woodleigh
runners.spotlight: Has gained last two wins over 1m2f at Redcar, including latest start; runner-up in most recent 1m attempt so he may be able to remain competitive.
runners.comment: Last two wins over 1m2f at Redcar, including latest start; has recent form over 1m

runners.name: Create
runners.spotlight: Gained sole turf win at Doncaster but returns here with a big doubt over her current form, highlighted by those duck eggs next to her name.
runners.comment: Has won at Doncaster but returns here with a big doubt over current form
runners.owner: Dixon, Wylam, M Baldry, J&s Harrod

runners.name: Whiskey Pete
runners.spotlight: Long time since his sole win and two duck eggs this term help to demonstrate a big doubt over his current form.
runners.comment: Two duck eggs this term reflect a big doubt over his current form

runners.name: Bizarre Law
runners.spotlight: Below par last weekend when bidding for a Doncaster 7f double; a possible threat if he rebounds but others are higher up the list.
runners.comment: Below par last weekend when bidding for a Doncaster 7f double

runners.name: Onslow Gardens
runners.spotlight: Has inconsistent form for current yard, aside from being placed a few times, and is still a maiden at the aged of six.
runners.comment: Has inconsistent form for current yard and remains a maiden aged six
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Back From Dubai
runners.spotlight: In-form 9yo who is a dual winner at Doncaster, including on penultimate start; creditable fourth at Leicester latest; in the mix.
runners.comment: In-form 9yo who is a dual winner at Doncaster, including on penultimate start

runners.name: Hachiman
runners.spotlight: AW winner for previous yard; inconsistent for new stable but ran encouragingly at Redcar (1m) in most recent turf attempt; could go well.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly in most recent turf attempt; could go well
runners.owner: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Rory's Gold
runners.spotlight: Maiden; best form on AW; made no impact returned to turf (7f) on seasonal debut; return to 1m may but he has something to prove.
runners.comment: Return to 1m may help but he still has something to prove on turf

runners.name: Yafaarr
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-15 but he gained the success off current mark, albeit on AW; good chance provided he's back in similar form.
runners.comment: Gained sole success off current mark; good chance if back in top form

runners.name: Mercurius Power
runners.spotlight: Usually races on AW these days and he never figured in most recent turf attempt; losing run is mounting up.
runners.comment: Usually races on AW these days; losing run is mounting up

runners.name: Goal Line
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has failed to back up debut effort; fitting of tongue-tie needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Has failed to back up debut effort; tongue-tie fitted

runners.name: Midnight's Dream
runners.spotlight: Scored twice off higher marks for previous yard but has poor claims on AW form for new stable; needs help from first-time visor.
runners.comment: Poor claims on AW form for new yard; needs help from first-time visor

runners.name: Sceptic
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf but went close in most recent attempt in this sphere; scored at Kempton (1m) last time; may well remain competitive.
runners.comment: Went close in most recent turf attempt, then went one better on AW; may remain competitive

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: Boston Max
runners.spotlight: He has left his 2yo form well behind him since gelded and handicapping, winning easily at Dundalk in April before dead-heating at Chepstow last month (both 5f); 6f should suit and he could have more in the tank.
runners.comment: Chasing a hat-trick after two 5f wins this spring; 6f should suit; contender

runners.name: Full Gas
runners.spotlight: Never threatened in a 7f handicap at Redcar four weeks ago but his two previous 6f runs were both encouraging enough; new headgear given a go.
runners.comment: Below par over 7f last time but earlier 6f efforts give him claims; tries new headgear

runners.name: Athenian Spirit
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but placed off this mark in his three runs this year (7f-1m, AW); absent since February and he has a bit more to prove both on turf and over 6f.
runners.comment: In good form over further on AW when last seen; more to prove over 6f on turf
runners.owner: Mpr Xxi, S Marris & Rathmoy Racing Vii

runners.name: Solar Cooper
runners.spotlight: One win from eight starts, namely a 6f handicap at Ripon last month when wearing cheekpieces for the first time; the headgear was absent when sixth back at Ripon 17 days ago and no surprise to see it back in place today; respected.
runners.comment: Sole success came on the one occasion he's worn cheekpieces; interesting with aids back on

runners.name: The Tunguska Event
runners.spotlight: Headgear and a switch to minor handicaps has seen him make good progress since November, winning for the third time when making all at Catterick last month (6f, good); up 3lb and he has other pace to contend with in this bigger field.
runners.comment: Three wins since moving into handicaps, the latest at Catterick 24 days ago; this tougher

runners.name: Cosmic Clarets
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a whimper but a gelding operation has bucked his ideas up and he has run well for second in two 6f handicaps this spring; likely to be bang there once again.
runners.comment: Two good second-place finishes (6f) this spring; should be bang there once again

runners.name: Dontwaste A Moment
runners.spotlight: Dropped to 5f at Newcastle 12 days ago and made all to register his first success; has a 4lb rise to overcome in a deeper race but he should be fine at 6f and is unexposed.
runners.comment: Made all in a 5f handicap at Newcastle 12 days ago; up 4lb in better race but unexposed

runners.name: Power Of Chora
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly on her handicap debut (6f, good) in April but she failed to build on it at Wolverhampton 12 days ago (7f); tongue-tie now left off and cheekpieces are tried.
runners.comment: Dropped 6lb after two handicap defeats; cheekpieces now tried; can do better

runners.name: Head For Freedom
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f claimer at Beverley last spring; on a good mark on his best handicap form and his latest Ripon fourth can be upgraded (not ideally positioned); each-way shout.
runners.comment: Latest fourth can be upgraded and he is slipping to a dangerous mark; each-way shout

runners.name: Henriette Ronner
runners.spotlight: 6f AW win for William Haggas in January (blinkered first time); unplaced in three runs for Martin Dunne and now starts out for another new yard (same owner) with headgear discarded.
runners.comment: 6f AW win in January for W Haggas; struggled for M Dunne; new yard discard the blinkers

runners.name: Hellcat
runners.spotlight: Back to form when third of six in a 6f handicap at Wolverhampton last month, her second run for Ross Burdon; she will need more to defy this mark but she now has something to build on.
runners.comment: Back to form when third at Wolverhampton last month; needs more to defy this mark though

runners.name: Antique Blue
runners.spotlight: Poor form for current stable and hard to recommend; cheekpieces replace a hood.
runners.comment: Poor form for current stable and hard to recommend
runners.owner: Miss K Padgett & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Come On Over
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; well treated on his 2yo best but yet to shine for David O'Meara this year; drops into a Class 6 but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden; hopes appear to rest on the drop into Class 6 company perking him up
runners.owner: Come On Over Syndicate & A H Yates Ltd

runners.name: Soul Warrior
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly at Newcastle in March, a run that was all the more disappointing as he had decisively won a Redcar nursery on his final 2yo start; still has time to come good and he sports a hood on his return from another 12 weeks off.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a nursery win at Redcar; low-key return in March (AW); now hooded
runners.owner: D Jenkins J Glover R O'Donnell J Short

runners.name: That's Random
runners.spotlight: He's not obviously well treated off this opening mark but has shown some ability among his first three starts (7f/6f, AW) and is bred to be better than this grade; no surprise if he takes a sizeable step forward.
runners.comment: Needs to leave his novice/maiden form behind but his pedigree suggests that is likely

races.race_name: The Takarazuka Kinen (Grade 1) (Turf)
Prix de Diane Longines (Group 1) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Diamond Necklace
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 as a juvenile when beating Green Spirit in the Marcel Boussac and had that rival further away in third when winning the Pouliches on last month's reappearance; everything about her pedigree and the way she has seen out her races suggests she'll stay 1m2f and, although her trainer has only won this race once, she's the one to beat.
runners.comment: Unbeaten filly who turned in best effort when winning last month's Pouliches; bred to stay
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Lilt
runners.spotlight: Fair form when winning in testing conditions at Doncaster on sole juvenile start and considerably better effort when winning Listed event over 1m at York on reappearance; there's enough in her pedigree to think she'll be effective at this trip and she's capable of better; interesting runner.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two starts, including Listed event at York on reappearance; should stay 1m2f

runners.name: Evolutionist
runners.spotlight: Has improved with every start and followed Group 3 Longchamp win in April with a fine second to True Love in the 1,000 Guineas; that race has worked out well (bar the below-par run of Venetian Lace in the Oaks); not a certainty on pedigree to appreciate this trip but has has plenty of class and she's a player if her stamina does hold out.
runners.comment: Has improved with every start and best effort in the 1,000 Guineas; stamina a worry

runners.name: Felicitas
runners.spotlight: Has improved significantly with every start, with her best effort when second to subsequent Oaks runner-up Legacy Link in last month's Musidora; just outstayed on good ground that day and it may be a similar story this time.
runners.comment: Best effort when second in the Musidora last time but may find one or two too good

runners.name: Inis Mor
runners.spotlight: Progressive juvenile who was well beaten in the face of a stiff task in the 1,000 Guineas but bounced back on her first run over 1m2f when winning Listed event at Goodwood; this demands a big step forward.
runners.comment: Back to winning ways on first run over 1m2f at Goodwood last time; plenty more needed

runners.name: Esna
runners.spotlight: Creditable fourth to Diamond Necklace in the Marcel Boussac in October and stepped up on reappearance form when winning Listed event over 1m2f at Newbury last time; more required to follow up from stall 11.
runners.comment: Matched pick of juvenile form when winning Listed event at Newbury last time; more needed

runners.name: Green Spirit
runners.spotlight: Fine second in the Marcel Boussac and, although further behind Diamond Necklace in the Pouliches, that effort can be marked up as she was drawn much wider and came from further back than the O'Brien runner; this draw should be more suitable given she usually races up with the pace and no surprise to see her go close over a trip she's bred to stay.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Marcel Boussac and better than the bare form in the Pouliches; place claims

runners.name: Moments Of Joy
runners.spotlight: Group 3-placed as a juvenile but below best on her reappearance at the Curragh and didn't get home when upped to this trip in the Musidora on here most recent start; considerably more needed and looks the likely pacemaker for stable companion Diamond Necklace.
runners.comment: Didn't improve for the step up to this trip in the Musidora and she faces a tough task
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Habibi
runners.spotlight: Trainer won this four times from 2009 to 2016 but, while this one has shown form bordering on smart in Listed and Group 2 company this spring, she'll have to raise her game by some way if she's to win on her first outing in Group 1 company.
runners.comment: Form bordering on smart but big step forward needed if she's to win in this company

runners.name: Pink Panthera
runners.spotlight: Capable sort who ran respectably behind the unbeaten Behrayna at Longchamp prior to beating a reliable yardstick in a Listed contest in the provinces last time; plenty more required.
runners.comment: Best effort when beating a reliable sort in Listed event last time; this demands more

runners.name: Evita
runners.spotlight: Has quickly made up into a smart sort and turned in her best effort when third in steadily run Group 2 Saint-Alary at Longchamp last time (in front of Habibi); Christophe Soumillon again rides but she needs to post a clear pb if she's to figure at the finish.
runners.comment: Best effort when third in steadily run Saint-Alary last time; good deal more needed
runners.owner: Bradley Thoroughbreds LLC, O Ghrghar & C & Y Lerner

races.race_name: Prix Pawneese Longines Presente Par RMC (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Prix Bertrand du Breuil Longines (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Elastic
runners.spotlight: Heavy ground Listed winner in March who turned in his best effort when a staying-on fifth in the Poulains; probably capable of better but shaped that day as though a step up in trip would suit so remains to be seen whether a potentially muddling event such as this will play to his strengths.
runners.comment: Best effort to finish fifth in the Poulains but a potentially messy race may not suit

runners.name: Sahlan
runners.spotlight: Turned in his best effort when winning the Group 1 Moulin at Longchamp on good to soft in September and not far off that level around sharper Del Mar on firm ground in the Breeders' Cup Mile; never threatened in the Lockinge but incurs no penalty and this Sussex Stakes entry is the one to beat in this lesser grade.
runners.comment: Group 1 Moulin winner; below best in the Lockinge but this represents a much easier test

runners.name: Remmooz
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two but made up for lost time last year by winning four of six starts, culminating in a Listed success on good to soft at Newbury in September; shaped as though retaining all his ability over this trip at Ascot on his reappearance and this prominent-racer is capable of better.
runners.comment: Shaped as though retaining all his ability on reappearance; claims back at 1m

runners.name: Matauri Bay
runners.spotlight: Debut winner at Leicester for Ralph Beckett in 2024 and turned in a smart performance behind Field Of Gold in that year's Solario; has improved with every run for Gerald Mosse and he posted a pb behind subsequent Diomed winner Seagulls Eleven in Listed event at Longchamp last time; a further step up is needed if he's to win this.
runners.comment: Not won since debut but posted his best effort last time (form franked); more needed

races.race_name: Prix du Lys Longines (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Rumoriste
runners.spotlight: Unexposed sort who turned in his best effort when winning a Listed event over this trip last time (clear with runner-up); this Arc entry looks the pick of the yard's two runners and he seems sure to give a good account.
runners.comment: Turned in best effort to win over this trip last time and has strong claims

runners.name: Chamali
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive sort who was allowed an easy lead and, although he tasted defeat for the first time, ran well in the Hocquart over 1m3f on good to soft last time; unlikely to be a problem with this trip and there should be more to come.
runners.comment: Lost unbeaten record in the Hocquart last time but ran well and there's more to come

runners.name: Space Waltz
runners.spotlight: Has improved with every run and turned in best effort when second in the Group 3 Noailles on his return in April; off since but should appreciate the step up to this trip and he's a player.
runners.comment: Progressive sort who should be suited by the step up to 1m4f; likely to run well

runners.name: Victoire Magique
runners.spotlight: Appreciated the step up to 1m7f when winning a conditions event at Angers on Monday; he'll need a career-best effort to follow up back in trip in this company.
runners.comment: Won conditions event over 1m7f at Angers on Monday; more to do back in trip in Group 3

races.race_name: Stockholms Stora Pris (Group 3) (Turf)
Sparkasse KolnBonn - 191st Union-Rennen (Group 2) (Colts) (Turf)
